events.description: Israeli Air Force launches 'wide-scale' strikes on Tehran using approximately 80 fighter jets dropping 230 munitions. Targets include IRGC Aerospace Force headquarters, oil storage depots at Shahran, Aghdasieh, and Karaj, and the Tehran refinery. 16 cargo aircraft used by IRGC Quds Force destroyed at Mehrabad Airport. At least 4 killed. First strikes on Iran's oil infrastructure since war began.
events.civilian_impact: Strikes on oil storage and refinery infrastructure caused environmental contamination; civilian areas affected by strikes on military targets

events.description: Tehran engulfed in toxic black smoke after overnight Israeli strikes on fuel depots cause fires to burn for hours. Iran's Red Crescent warns residents to stay indoors due to acid rain containing hydrocarbons, sulphur and nitrogen oxides. Oil-tainted black rainfall reported across the city causing chemical burn risk.
events.civilian_impact: Toxic black smoke engulfed Tehran; Iran's Red Crescent warned residents to stay indoors due to acid rain containing hydrocarbons, sulphur and nitrogen oxides; oil-tainted black rainfall reported across the city

events.description: Israeli Navy strikes Ramada hotel in Beirut's Rawche seaside area, killing 5 senior IRGC Quds Force commanders including Majid Hassini (Lebanon Corps finance), Ali Reza Bi-Azar (Lebanon Corps intelligence chief), Ahmad Rasouli (Palestine Corps intelligence chief), and Hossein Ahmadlou (Lebanon Corps intelligence). 4 killed per Lebanese health ministry, 10 wounded.
events.source: Times of Israel, France 24, Al Jazeera

events.description: Iran's Assembly of Experts elects Mojtaba Khamenei (57), son of slain Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, as the third Supreme Leader of the Islamic Republic. IRGC and senior officials pledge allegiance. Trump calls him 'lightweight' and demands input in choosing Iran's leader. IDF issues statement in Persian warning it will 'pursue every successor.'
events.source: NPR, NBC News, CBS News, Al Jazeera

events.description: Iranian ballistic missile with cluster warhead strikes central Israel, hitting Tel Aviv and Petah Tikva. 6-7 wounded including a man in his 40s in serious condition in Tel Aviv. Multiple fragment impact sites reported. Sixth Iranian salvo of the day targeting Israel.
events.source: Jerusalem Post, Haaretz, Times of Israel
events.civilian_impact: 6-7 wounded including a man in his 40s in serious condition in Tel Aviv

events.description: Iranian missile strikes residential area in Al-Kharj governorate, Saudi Arabia, killing 2 foreign workers (Indian and Bangladeshi nationals) and wounding 12 Bangladeshi residents. First civilian fatalities in Saudi Arabia since the war began. Saudi defense ministry reports intercepting 15 Iranian drones near Riyadh.
events.civilian_impact: 2 foreign workers killed (Indian and Bangladeshi nationals); 12 Bangladeshi residents wounded

events.description: Pentagon confirms 7th US service member killed in the conflict. The soldier died from injuries sustained during an Iranian attack on a base in Saudi Arabia on March 1. Total US military deaths now at 7 since Operation Epic Fury began.

Iranian drone attack damages water desalination plant in Bahrain and causes fire at Mina Salman seaport facility. 3 people injured. Attack raises fears about targeting of civilian water infrastructure in Gulf states.
events.civilian_impact: Water desalination plant damaged; fire at Mina Salman seaport facility; 3 people injured

events.description: UAE reports new Iranian barrage of 17 ballistic missiles and 117 drones. UAE air defense intercepts 16 missiles; 17th falls into sea. Combined with earlier wave of 15 ballistic missiles and 119 drones intercepted on March 7. Debris and shrapnel from interceptions cause injuries in populated areas. Total UAE casualties since war began: 4 killed, 112 injured.
events.source: Gulf News, The National, Al Jazeera
events.civilian_impact: Debris and shrapnel from interceptions caused injuries in populated areas

events.description: At least 41 killed and 40 wounded in Israeli air-and-ground raids on Nabi Chit (Nabi Sheet) in Lebanon's eastern Bekaa Valley. Israeli helicopter-borne commandos land in the town in an overnight operation reportedly aimed at locating remains of missing IAF navigator Ron Arad. Fierce ground battles reported between IDF and Hezbollah.
events.source: Al Jazeera, France 24, Middle East Monitor
events.civilian_impact: At least 41 killed and 40 wounded in Israeli air-and-ground raids

events.description: Israel's strikes on 30 Iranian fuel depots go 'far beyond' what the US expected, sparking the first significant US-Israel disagreement since the war began. US officials express concern about escalation and environmental damage. Oil prices reach $100/barrel for first time since July 2022.

UN declares the war a 'major humanitarian emergency' as nearly 25 million people in affected regions are already refugees, internally displaced, or recent returnees. UNHCR mobilizes across region. 517,000+ displaced in Lebanon; 84,000 in collective shelters. Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed, disrupting humanitarian supply routes to Gaza, Yemen, and Somalia.
events.civilian_impact: Nearly 25 million people in affected regions are already refugees, internally displaced, or recent returnees; 517,000+ displaced in Lebanon; 84,000 in collective shelters

events.description: Reports confirm China secretly supplied Iran with approximately $5 billion in weapons via an oil-for-weapons deal, including HQ-9B surface-to-air missile systems, HQ-16B SAMs, FN-6 MANPADS, CM-302 anti-ship missiles, JY-26 radar systems, and Sunflower-200 kamikaze drones. The deal was signed covertly and accelerated after the June 2025 Israel-Iran 12-day war.
events.source: Global Defense Corp, The National, Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post

events.description: Chinese-made HQ-9B air defense systems deployed around Tehran fail to intercept US-Israeli strikes during opening hours of Operation Epic Fury. US/Israeli forces suppressed Iranian radar and data links first, rendering the SAMs ineffective. Iran's multi-layered air defense — including Chinese HQ-9B, Russian S-300, and domestic systems — was overwhelmed within hours. Analysts say the failure should 'worry the Chinese Communist Party' about their export weapons' combat performance.
events.source: The Economist, India TV News, Global Defense Corp, People's News Today

events.description: US submarine sinks Iranian warship in Indian Ocean south of Sri Lanka. More than 100 military personnel aboard the vessel killed.
events.civilian_impact: 100+ military personnel aboard vessel

events.description: Iran's military targets headquarters of Iranian Kurdish forces in northern Iraq, stepping up strikes on Kurdish opposition groups.
Strikes on water desalination plants in Bahrain. Attacks on non-military water infrastructure described as 'serious escalation' by analysts
events.civilian_impact: Water desalination plants attacked, civilian water supply disrupted

events.description: US Embassy in Baghdad targeted as Iraq gets drawn deeper into regional war. Trump administration pressures Iraqi leaders to distance themselves from Iran.

Israeli strikes on the town of Seir al-Gharbiya in southern Lebanon kill 19 people, most of them women and children according to Lebanese National News Agency. Rescue operations underway as buildings collapse. Part of intensified southern Lebanon campaign on day nine of the war.
events.source: The New Arab, Lebanese National News Agency
events.civilian_impact: 19 killed including women and children; buildings collapsed; rescue operations ongoing

events.description: Israeli strikes kill at least 20 people across multiple towns in southern Lebanon as Israel renews assault on day nine. Targets include residential areas and infrastructure. Death toll in Lebanon passes 394 killed in one week including 83 children, 42 women, and 9 rescue workers, per Lebanese Health Ministry.
events.civilian_impact: 20+ killed across multiple southern towns; 517,000+ displaced; schools in Beirut converted to shelters

events.description: Two Israeli strikes hit Ain al-Helweh Palestinian refugee camp on the outskirts of Sidon, the largest Palestinian refugee camp in Lebanon. Smoke rises from the camp. Casualties reported but numbers not yet confirmed. Camp houses approximately 120,000 Palestinian refugees.
events.source: Times of Israel, The New Arab
events.civilian_impact: Palestinian refugee camp struck; 120,000 refugees at risk; smoke and damage reported

events.description: Israel strikes town of Ghaziyeh in southern Lebanon. At least one person rescued alive from rubble. Evacuation orders issued for Arnoun, Yohmor, Zrariyeh East, and Zrariyeh West. Residents ordered to move north of Nabatieh governorate immediately.
events.source: Al Jazeera, Lebanese National News Agency
events.civilian_impact: People trapped under rubble; evacuation orders for multiple villages; mass displacement

events.description: Israeli strikes hit Beirut and southern Lebanon, killing 10 people amid threats against Iranian officials hiding in Lebanon. Israel issues warnings to Lebanese civilians to evacuate areas where Hezbollah operates. Strikes target buildings in Beirut's Dahiyeh suburbs.
events.civilian_impact: 10 killed; civilian buildings struck in Dahiyeh

events.description: Israel kills more than 100 Lebanese in a single day as it commands Beirut residents to flee. Massive strikes on multiple locations across Lebanon mark the deadliest day of the conflict for Lebanon. Buildings devastated across the country as death toll rises sharply.
events.civilian_impact: 100+ killed in single day; buildings devastated; mass evacuation orders for Beirut; death toll rises sharply

events.description: Lebanese Health Ministry confirms cumulative death toll from Israeli attacks on Lebanon reaches 394 people killed in one week, including 83 children, 42 women, and 9 rescue workers. Over 1,200 wounded. 517,000+ people registered as displaced. Israeli military claims to have struck over 600 Hezbollah targets and killed 200 Hezbollah members. Ground operations continue in southern Lebanon.
events.source: Al Jazeera, RTE, Manila Times, Euronews
events.civilian_impact: 394 killed including 83 children, 42 women, 9 rescue workers; 1,200+ wounded; 517,000+ displaced; schools converted to shelters

events.description: International Women's Day marches in multiple countries denounce US-Israeli war on Iran. Thousands of demonstrators in Spain and other countries take stand against the war.
Iranian attacks killed one person and injured 32 in Kuwait
events.civilian_impact: 1 killed, 32 injured

events.description: Death toll from Israeli attacks on Lebanon rises to at least 217 people killed, with 798 wounded and approximately 95,000 displaced as of March 6, 2026. Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam warns consequences may be 'unprecedented' at humanitarian and political levels. Israel launched air strikes on southern Lebanese towns including Srifa, Aita al-Shaab, Touline, as-Sawana, and Majdal Selem.
events.civilian_impact: 217 killed, 798 wounded, 95,000 displaced

events.description: Cumulative death toll from Iranian strikes and US-Israeli attacks: preliminary figures are 1,255 dead in Iran, at least 13 in Israel, eight US soldiers and 14 killed in Gulf states.

Death toll in Iran reaches 555 as US-Israeli strikes continue. At least 35 people killed in southern Iran's Fars province. More than 20 people killed in Niloofar Square in Tehran. At least two people killed in Sanandaj with several residential buildings destroyed. US-Israeli forces dropped six missiles on different parts of Sanandaj including densely populated neighbourhoods.
events.civilian_impact: 555+ killed including civilians in residential areas

events.description: Death toll in Iran surpasses 1,000 as Israel-US strikes continue. Israeli attacks on Wednesday struck Tehran, Qom, western Iran, and Isfahan province. Israeli attacks also damaged residential units. Israel struck buildings belonging to Basij paramilitary force and Iran's internal security command. Death toll reaches 1,045.
events.civilian_impact: 1,045+ killed, civilians bearing brunt of attacks, residential areas damaged

events.description: Israeli or US strikes on Shajareh Tayebeh primary school in Minab and adjacent IRGC compound. Multiple sources report 168 killed including students, with satellite imagery showing multiple simultaneous strikes.
events.civilian_impact: 168 killed including primary school students, school struck multiple times

events.description: Iranian drone struck a Kuwaiti government high-rise building housing the Public Institution for Social Security. Flames tore through the building resulting in material damage. Attack occurred in early hours of Sunday.
events.civilian_impact: Material damage to government building

events.description: Hezbollah launches strikes on Israel in response to the killing of Iranian supreme leader Ali Khamenei. In response, Israel launches strikes in Beirut, the Lebanese capital.

Israeli strikes on 30 Iranian fuel depots. US officials express concern that strikes go 'far beyond' what was expected, sparking first significant US-Israel disagreement since war began. Environmental damage from fuel depot strikes noted. Oil prices reach $100/barrel for first time since July 2022.
events.civilian_impact: Environmental damage from fuel depot strikes; toxic smoke over Tehran

events.description: Explosions reported in the Iranian capital as war with US and Israel enters fifth day.
Satellite images show Iran school strike hit more buildings than earlier reported, indicating expanded damage from airstrikes.
Iran school and nearby military base struck multiple times according to satellite imagery analysis.
events.civilian_impact: School struck, civilian impact likely

events.description: US missile struck military base near Iran school, according to BBC video analysis
events.civilian_impact: strike near school

events.description: Iranian ballistic missile intercepted by NATO air defences over southern Turkey. Turkish Ministry of National Defence reports no casualties or damage reported.
Iran launches missiles at Israel and US bases as part of escalating response
Airstrikes intensify over Tehran as Iran's missile barrages decline in frequency and scale
Israeli attacks on Iranian oil facilities. WHO chief raises alarm about damage to Iranian petroleum facilities, with particular risk to children and the elderly.
events.civilian_impact: Children and elderly at particular risk from damage to petroleum facilities

events.description: Gulf states weigh response to Iranian strikes; escalating tensions over 'red lines'
events.civilian_impact: Regional diplomatic tensions; potential for expanded conflict

events.description: Joint U.S.-Israeli 'Operation Epic Fury' officially launched, targeting military and security targets in Iran
CENTCOM reports US targets numerous Iranian military assets in first 24 hours of Operation Epic Fury
Iran launches strikes on the Gulf states, described as 'burning the bridges of good neighbourliness'
Trump's strikes on Iran could cost American economy as much as $210 billion, according to top budget expert analysis
Operation Epic Fury analysis of impact on Iran's nuclear program and military capabilities
events.source: CSIS | Center for Strategic and International Studies

events.description: Analysis indicates potential incorrect military targeting during Operation Epic Fury ('We Bombed the Wrong Target')
Fresh graves and angry slogans observed at cemetery in Tehran following Israeli strikes
events.civilian_impact: Multiple civilian deaths inferred from graves

events.description: Israeli Forces raid new area in southern Lebanon and bombard near Beirut
events.civilian_impact: Unknown civilian casualties from bombard near Beirut

events.description: Iranian missile headed towards Turkey destroyed, according to Defense Ministry reports
US Secretary of War Pete Hegseth declares 'Iran Will Not Win' and praises 'Devastating' strikes of Operation Epic Fury
Iran's Assembly of Experts confirms Mojtaba Khamenei (son of slain Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei) as new Supreme Leader to lead Iran through ongoing conflict
US military announces attacks on Iran will continue for weeks and intensify according to Pentagon statements
France announces deployment of large naval force to the Middle East in response to US-Israel war on Iran.
Five Iranian women footballers targeted; Trump states Australia has 'taken care of' them following criticism for not singing national anthem at AFC Women's Asian Cup.
events.civilian_impact: Five Iranian female athletes subjected to unspecified action by Australian authorities

events.description: Iranian missile intercepted over Turkey according to Erdogan's warning against provocative steps
Explosions heard across Doha with sirens sounding in Bahrain as Iran strikes Gulf neighbours
events.civilian_impact: Explosions and sirens indicate widespread area impact

events.description: Iranian missile intercepted over Qatar
Macron announces 'purely defensive' mission to reopen Strait of Hormuz with France and allies
Hezbollah reports clash with overnight Israeli raid in east Lebanon
US and Gulf states turn to Ukraine to counter Iranian drone attacks
G7 leaders to discuss release of strategic oil reserves as crisis looms
Gulf countries targeted by fresh Iranian attacks as repatriation still underway
events.civilian_impact: Repatriation operations underway

events.description: Half a million displaced in Lebanon amid intensifying Israeli strikes
events.civilian_impact: 500,000 people displaced

events.description: Iran's foreign minister Abbas Araghchi mocks US operation, brands it 'Operation Epic Mistake' and warns of Iranian surprises in store
Oil prices soar as Iran names a new supreme leader and launches more attacks
Iran launches more attacks as part of escalated response following new supreme leader appointment

US forces have struck nearly 2,000 targets in Iran since the war began, eliminating several top Iranian officials including Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei in Tehran. Attacks targeted nuclear facilities, civilian areas, oil refineries and desalination plants.
events.civilian_impact: Attacks on civilian areas reported

events.description: Iran reports 1,255 people killed in US-Israeli attacks, with majority being civilians.
events.civilian_impact: 1,255 people killed, mostly civilians

events.description: Five members of Iranian women's football team granted humanitarian visas and asylum in Australia after escaping government minders following tournament, with fears of punishment if they returned home.
events.civilian_impact: Five female athletes granted asylum due to political pressure

events.description: Analysis questions US endgame in Iran war, noting contradictory positions articulated by President Trump on war objectives as missile strikes mount.
Ayatollah Ali Khamenei assassinated on first day of US-Israeli war, killed in initial strikes during Operation Epic Fury
events.civilian_impact: Supreme Leader assassinated

events.description: Trump states military campaign against Iran is 'ahead of schedule' and 'war pretty much complete'
Houthis and Hezbollah pledge backing for Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei
Trump and Putin discuss Iran war during phone call
US strikes on Iran may have transformed Iran from state with latent nuclear capability into one with nuclear grievance
Trump suggests short-term oil price spike from Iran war is 'small price to pay' for peace
Canada's pro-U.S. stance in Iran attack carries security risks, experts say
Trump administration designates Sudanese Muslim Brotherhood as 'terrorist' group, accusing it of receiving support from Iran's IRGC and carrying out violence against civilians.
Mojtaba Khamenei confirmed as new Supreme Leader of Iran; Iranian state television reports he is 'janbaz' (wounded by enemy) in unspecified incident at unknown time and location.

Trump administration and Israeli officials reportedly discussing seizure of Kharg Island, Iran's primary oil export terminal handling roughly 90% of Iran's crude oil exports, as strategic shift in conflict.
Israeli airstrike struck Sainte Therese area in Beirut's southern suburbs following evacuation warning; Israeli drones also flew over Baabda, Hadath and other southern suburbs areas.
events.civilian_impact: Evacuation warning issued; casualty information not yet available

events.description: Trump states war in Iran is 'very complete, pretty much,' claiming Iran has no navy, no communications, and no air force; indicates US military campaign will be ending soon amid Republican concerns about prolonged conflict.
Iran launches new wave of missiles and drones targeting Gulf nations in retaliation for US-Israeli attacks
events.civilian_impact: Gulf airspace shut, oil production disrupted

events.description: Iranian attack on Bahrain's Bapco refinery complex, fire reported at refinery installation
events.civilian_impact: Refinery fire, force majeure declared by state oil company Bapco, oil supply disrupted

events.description: Mojtaba Khamenei, 56, appointed as new supreme leader of Iran following death of father Ali Khamenei; signs missile with message 'At your service, Sayyid Mojtaba' directed at Israel
New supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei launches first strikes within hours of appointment, targeting Israel
Australia announces deployment of specialist surveillance aircraft and air-to-air missiles to UAE to protect civilians from Iranian attacks
events.civilian_impact: Protection measure for Australian citizens under threat from Iranian attack

events.description: Iran's Prosecutor General's Office warns overseas citizens of asset seizure and death penalty for supporting or cooperating with Israel, US, or affiliated groups
events.civilian_impact: Iranians abroad threatened with capital punishment and asset confiscation

events.description: Oil prices surge following Iran's appointment of new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei and continued Iranian attacks; Nikkei 225 index plunges up to 7% in early Monday trading
events.civilian_impact: Economic disruption, inflation concerns, reduced consumer spending expected

events.description: Strait of Hormuz operations disrupted; cables in strait carry 37% of global internet traffic, threatening data services and connectivity
events.civilian_impact: Global internet connectivity threatened, impact on data services and cloud infrastructure worldwide

events.description: Trump claims US and Israel have struck 5,000 targets since war began on February 28, calling past 10 days a 'short-term excursion' and stating war will be over 'very soon'
Oil prices tumble sharply after Trump claims US-Israel war on Iran could end 'very soon', with Brent crude falling from four-year highs above $100 per barrel
Oil prices continue to surge beyond $115 per barrel amid Iran war, triggering stark sell-offs across leading stock markets and raising global stagflation concerns
Hudson Institute analysis discusses Iran's declining military capabilities and emerging strategy as Operation Epic Fury conflict nears its second week
Iran's new Supreme Leader receives backing from Houthi movement and Hezbollah amid ongoing US-Israeli war
Shells fired from Lebanon land west of Syrian capital Damascus, according to Syria's military statement
40 people killed in Tehran according to live war coverage
events.civilian_impact: 40 killed in Tehran

events.description: Israeli military targeting Hezbollah's financial arm al-Qard Al-Hasan in Lebanon with ground forces launching focused raids against infrastructure
Israeli forces used white phosphorus incendiary shells in strikes on residential areas in Lebanese village, violation of international law according to Human Rights Watch
events.civilian_impact: White phosphorus used on residential areas

events.description: Iran fired more drones and missiles across the Persian Gulf and beyond in response to US and Israeli airstrikes after choosing Mojtaba Khamenei as new supreme leader
Dozens wounded in Bahrain as result of Iranian strikes on Gulf Arab states
events.civilian_impact: Dozens wounded

events.description: Iran names hard-line Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei (son of late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei) as new supreme leader, signaling continuation of hardline rule
events.source: AP News via Mankato Free Press

events.description: Bombs hit military bases, government buildings, oil and water installations, hotels and at least one school in Iran during 10-day war
events.source: AP News via Mankato Free Press
events.civilian_impact: School hit among other civilian infrastructure

events.description: Iran reports cumulative death toll of 1,255 people killed in US-Israeli attacks, mostly civilians
events.civilian_impact: 1,255 reported killed, mostly civilians (cumulative)

events.description: Lebanese parliament extended its term by two years due to escalating US-Israel war with Iran and Israeli attacks on Lebanon
US sinks Iranian warship amid Gulf shipping crisis; tankers stranded for fifth day
Iran uses kamikaze drone boats for first time in Middle East war
US will provide insurance for ships in Gulf amid Iranian attacks
IAEA confirms buildings damaged at Iran's Natanz nuclear facility
IAEA confirms entrances to Iran's Natanz enrichment plant were bombed
Trump suggests without evidence that Iran struck elementary school
events.civilian_impact: Alleged strike on elementary school

events.description: US conducts military buildup near Iran, including deployment of 2 aircraft carriers
Maritime warning zone established in place as US and Israel launch 'massive' strikes on Iran
Container ship attacked in Strait of Hormuz; crew rescued
events.civilian_impact: Merchant vessel targeted; crew evacuated

events.description: Israeli military recently fired white phosphorus munitions over residential areas in southern Lebanon, drawing international attention and raising humanitarian and legal concerns
events.civilian_impact: White phosphorus deployed in populated residential areas

events.description: Trump dismisses threat from Iranian official Ali Larijani, who warned that Trump would 'pay the price' for ongoing US-Israeli military campaign against Iran
Officially 11 Israelis killed in Iranian strikes since war began on February 28; actual number and scale of Iron Dome penetrations unknown
Iranians rally in downtown Tehran to show support for Mojtaba Khamenei as new supreme leader

Explosions heard across Tehran as Israel continued strikes on Iranian targets; blasts shook buildings and disrupted power in parts of capital and nearby cities. Iran responded with Operation True Promise 4 missile barrage using heavier missiles
events.civilian_impact: Power disruptions across Tehran and nearby cities; residents reported repeated explosions

events.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps stated Tehran, not Washington, will determine when war ends; rejected Trump's timeline claims. IRGC spokesperson claimed all US military infrastructure in Persian Gulf destroyed and US ammunition running out
Israel warned Beirut residents to evacuate ahead of planned strikes on buildings linked to Al-Qard al-Hasan financial association in southern suburbs, accused of financing Hezbollah
events.civilian_impact: Evacuation warning issued to residents of southern suburbs

events.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps claimed it destroyed 10 advanced US radar systems across the region and downed several expensive drones during conflict. IRGC warned of impending USS Gerald R. Ford arrival in Strait of Hormuz

Iranian military official stated Iran would block oil exports to countries it considers enemies or their allies; would not allow export of 'a single litre of oil' until further notice. Iran has closed Strait of Hormuz affecting global economy
events.civilian_impact: Global oil supply disruption; economic impact on international trade

events.description: Trump says military operation 'Epic Fury' in Iran will end 'very soon'
UAE and Qatar aligned that Iran 'has crossed a red line' as airstrikes continue
France is sending a large naval force to the Middle East amid US-Iran conflict

Israeli military struck Al-Qard al-Hasan financial institution (Hezbollah-affiliated charity) branches in Beirut, including location in Bir al-Abed area in Haret Hreik and near Beirut international airport

US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth states that US military has suffered 7 confirmed deaths in Iran war, with expectation of more casualties; references six army reservists killed in Iranian drone strike on US base in Kuwait one week prior
Iran declares it will attack any ship attempting to pass through the Strait of Hormuz
IAEA claims no radiological risk after strikes on Iran
Analysis of Iran's layered missile and mine defenses against US carriers in Strait of Hormuz
US President Donald Trump threatened to escalate attacks on Iran if it disrupted global oil supplies, saying 'they'll get hit at a much, much harder level.'
Hezbollah forces in Lebanon fired artillery shells towards Syrian army positions in the town of Saraghaya, located west of Damascus, according to SANA news agency.
events.civilian_impact: Syrian positions targeted; civilian impact unknown

events.description: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese announced decision to send military support including missiles to the United Arab Emirates amid the US-Israel war on Iran.
Iran named hard-line Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, son of late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, as the new supreme leader, signaling no letup in the war with the United States and Israel.
US President Trump stated in an interview with Hebrew media that he and Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu would make a 'mutual decision' on when to end the war with Iran.

French President Emmanuel Macron announced France would deploy almost a dozen warships and is preparing a 'defensive' mission to reopen the Strait of Hormuz to escort container ships and tankers after the hottest phase of the conflict ends.

Approximately 2,000 Iranian protesters marched through London from Whitehall to the Iranian embassy in Kensington, chanting 'Trump and Bibi, thank you' in celebration of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's death and calling for the British government to proscribe the IRGC as a terrorist organization.

Israeli military struck the Al-Qard al-Hasan financial institution (Hezbollah-affiliated charity) in the Bir al-Abed area in the southern neighbourhood of Haret Hreik in Beirut, and another branch along the road to Beirut's international airport.
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi told PBS News that talking or negotiating with the US is no longer on the agenda, citing US attacks despite previous negotiation progress.
U.S. Forces destroyed 17 Iranian warships and one submarine in Operation Epic Fury strikes.
Trump says US military 'excursion' in Iran will be 'short-term'.
America destroyed Iran's Shahid Soleimani warship.
Lebanon's parliament voted to extend its term by two years, postponing scheduled May 2026 parliamentary elections due to ongoing conflict involving Israel and security concerns.
events.civilian_impact: Displacement and security concerns cited

events.description: Iran chose Mojtaba Khamenei, hardline son of assassinated Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, as new supreme leader, signaling Tehran won't back down in ongoing Middle East war.
Shahed drone struck a British air base on the southern coast of Cyprus. First drone attack of the war on European territory.
events.civilian_impact: British air base targeted

events.description: US spent almost $6 billion in first week of war on Iran.

US President Donald Trump announced his administration will lift some sanctions on oil-producing countries to keep energy prices down amid the US and Israel's war on Iran. Crude oil prices plunged below $90 a barrel following an earlier surge to almost $120 in a 24-hour price swing.
events.civilian_impact: None

events.description: US President Donald Trump threatened Iran with massive retaliation, warning 'Death, Fire, and Fury will reign upon them' if Iran continues to block oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz. Trump threatened Iran would be struck 'twenty times harder' and said the US would target 'easily destroyable targets that will make it virtually impossible for Iran to ever be built back, as a Nation, again.'
events.civilian_impact: None

events.description: Hezbollah said its fighters carried out several attacks on Israeli troops in southern Lebanon, including an ambush on soldiers on the outskirts of Khiam. Hezbollah claimed the attack struck three Israeli Merkava tanks which were 'seen burning'. The group also reported firing rockets at Israeli forces in Merkaba, conducting artillery strikes near Aitaroun, and attacking Israeli troops in the Kahil Heights near Maroun al-Ras.
events.civilian_impact: None reported

events.description: The Islamic Resistance in Iraq claimed its fighters carried out 37 operations against 'enemy bases' in Iraq and across the region over the past 24 hours. The pro-Iran armed group claimed the attacks involved dozens of drones and missiles.
events.civilian_impact: Unknown

events.description: The Guardian reported that hospitals, UNESCO world heritage sites, and desalination plants have been attacked by the US and Israel during the second week of the war on Iran. Rising death tolls reported as Iranians express fear and uncertainty about the conflict.
events.civilian_impact: Hospitals attacked, UNESCO heritage sites damaged, desalination plants struck; rising death tolls reported

events.description: The Pentagon is moving parts of the THAAD air defense system from South Korea to the Middle East, according to the Washington Post. The US is also using its stockpile of Patriot missiles in the Indian and Pacific Oceans. Pentagon moves are not due to weapon shortage but in anticipation of Iran escalating its attacks.
events.civilian_impact: None

events.description: A second ballistic missile launched from Iran was intercepted in Turkish airspace by NATO defense systems. This marked the second interception within five days. Turkey's defense ministry confirmed the missile entered Turkish airspace before being neutralized by NATO's air and missile defense assets in the eastern Mediterranean.
events.civilian_impact: None

events.description: US President Donald Trump had a call with Russian President Vladimir Putin to discuss the war in Iran and other issues. According to the Kremlin, it was a 'frank and businesslike' conversation that lasted about an hour. Heavy US and Israeli bombardment of Iran continued for more than a week while Iran launched more attacks on Israel and Gulf countries.
events.civilian_impact: None

events.description: Missile strike on school in Iran; photos analyzed by New York Times suggest US-made missile was used in deadly strike
events.civilian_impact: School struck; civilians killed

events.description: Israeli attacks on fuel depots in Tehran caused massive explosions and fires burning for hours, creating toxic smoke cloud over city of 10 million people
events.civilian_impact: Toxic black raindrops with hydrocarbon compounds, sulfur, nitrogen oxide fell on Tehran; residents reported breathing difficulties

events.description: Iran launched more attacks on Israel and Gulf countries in response to US and Israeli bombardment; Iranian state TV announced new Supreme Leader appointment as defiant response
CENTCOM reports US strikes over 5,000 Iranian targets and destroys 50 vessels in 10 days of Operation Epic Fury
B-1 and B-52 bombers pour into Europe for continued bomber operations over Iran
France announces naval build-up of dozen vessels in Eastern Mediterranean, Red Sea, and potentially Strait of Hormuz to establish defensive escort mission for commercial ships and tankers
War has killed at least 1,698 people throughout the Middle East; vast majority killed in Iran
events.civilian_impact: At least 1,698 people killed throughout Middle East; widespread regional impact with bombing and ground skirmishes in every country in region

events.description: Iranian missile or drone has already struck territory belonging to NATO member state
One-year-old girl killed in Tehran residential building hit by US and Israeli missiles. Body recovered from rubble.
events.civilian_impact: 1-year-old infant killed, residential building destroyed

events.description: US missile strike destroyed Hafez Khomeni School in central Khomeyn and damaged surrounding residential area
events.civilian_impact: School destroyed, residential homes damaged, civilian access to education disrupted

events.description: Iran named hard-line Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei to succeed his late father as supreme leader, signaling no letup in war
events.civilian_impact: Leadership succession during active conflict

events.description: Israeli strike killed Iran's late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and his son Mojtaba's wife Zahra Haddad Adel at start of war on February 28
events.civilian_impact: High-ranking political figures and family member killed

events.description: Israeli fighter jets bombarded southern outskirts of Beirut, Lebanese capital, producing huge explosions throughout the city
events.civilian_impact: Major population center targeted, civilians in capital affected

events.civilian_impact: Nearly 400 people killed, including over 80 children, in Lebanon conflict as of Sunday

events.description: Israel targeted Hezbollah's financial arm al-Qard Al-Hasan with ground forces launching focused raids against the group's infrastructure in Lebanon
events.civilian_impact: Hezbollah financial operations targeted

events.description: Human Rights Watch accused Israeli forces of using white phosphorus incendiary shells in strikes on residential areas in Lebanon
events.civilian_impact: Residential areas hit with incendiary weapons, potential civilian injuries

events.civilian_impact: US citizens evacuated from southeastern Turkey

events.civilian_impact: Expansion of military capability for strikes

events.description: UK Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer approved defensive US actions against Iranian targets from British bases
events.civilian_impact: UK involvement in military operations

events.description: UAE former envoy Hussain Hassan Mirza stated UAE has no intention of being pulled into Iran-Israel conflict and will not permit its land for military action by either side
events.civilian_impact: Regional diplomacy and neutrality declarations

events.description: Australia deploying long-range reconnaissance aircraft and air-to-air missiles to help Gulf countries defend against Iranian attacks; 12 countries across region from Cyprus to Gulf targeted
events.civilian_impact: International military involvement in regional conflict
