events.civilian_impact: 165 children killed at elementary school, school directly struck

events.description: Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei named as new supreme leader of Iran in succession after his father's assassination

Iran warns commercial shipping to exercise extreme caution through Strait of Hormuz; Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi warns of potential US strikes as an 'epic mistake' and signals Iran prepared to retaliate if oil and nuclear infrastructure attacked

South Korean President Lee Jae Myung announces his government cannot prevent the United States from redeploying weapons stationed in South Korea to the Middle East; reports indicate US Patriot missile defense systems may have been moved from Osan Air Base to American bases in Saudi Arabia and UAE
Oil prices surge following Iran's appointment of new supreme leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei and continued Iranian attacks on Israel and Gulf countries
Traffic dried up in the Strait of Hormuz since the Iran war began
Israeli warplanes bombed Shahran oil depot north-east of Tehran and Shahr-e fuel depot; both continued to burn two days after attack
events.civilian_impact: Iran's environmental agency warned of toxic chemicals, potential acid rain, damage to skin and lungs; five fossil fuel installations bombed around Tehran

events.description: Iranian Navy cleared out of Arabian Gulf according to CENTCOM chief
US calls on vessels to keep clear of Strait of Hormuz and surrounding waters amid military escalation
National Committee for International Humanitarian Law affirmed that targeting Qatari territory violates international law
actors: Qatar National Committee for International Humanitarian Law
events.civilian_impact: Qatari territory targeted in violation of international law

events.description: Latest death toll update on US-Israel war on Iran provided
Trump and Putin discuss Iran war during a phone call according to Kremlin
Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu promises 'many surprises' in next phase of war and renews assault on Lebanon
New airstrikes reported battering Tehran

Minab primary school bombing killed scores of people, mostly seven- to 12-year-old girls; described by UNESCO as 'grave violation' of international law; worst mass killing of US-Israeli war on Iran so far
events.civilian_impact: Scores of seven- to 12-year-old girls killed; described as worst mass killing of war

events.description: Hezbollah fired rockets towards Israel in response to killing of Iranian supreme leader Ali Khamenei on first day of US-Israeli attacks on Iran
Israeli retaliation following Hezbollah rocket fire; Israel conducted large-scale air raids on Lebanon and ground troop incursions killing at least 486 people according to Lebanese health ministry
events.civilian_impact: At least 486 people killed in large-scale air raids and ground incursions

events.description: Senior Iranian official Kamal Kharazi, foreign policy adviser to Supreme Leader, tells CNN that Iran is prepared for a long war with US and willing to continue attacking Gulf countries to convince Trump to step back; rules out diplomacy and says war will only end through economic pain
Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa declares support for Lebanese President Joseph Aoun in effort to disarm Hezbollah; Syrian army bolstered troop deployment
Australia grants asylum to Iranian soccer players
A 29-year-old woman killed and eight people injured when a residential building in Bahrain's capital Manama was hit in an Iranian attack
events.civilian_impact: 1 woman killed, 8 injured in residential building strike

events.description: Saudi Arabia, Kuwait and United Arab Emirates intercept drones and missiles from Iran

Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, 26, of Glendale, Kentucky, died of wounds sustained in Iranian missile and drone attack on Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia on March 1, 2026. He is the seventh U.S. service member confirmed killed in the war.
Israel warns Lebanon residents south of Litani River to evacuate immediately, stating air strikes are ongoing and the military is operating 'with great force in the area'
events.civilian_impact: Forced displacement threat to residents south of Litani River

events.description: France announces deployment of approximately a dozen naval vessels, including its nuclear-powered aircraft carrier Charles de Gaulle strike group, to the Mediterranean, Red Sea and potentially the Strait of Hormuz as defensive support to allies
events.region: Mediterranean, Red Sea, Strait of Hormuz

events.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio defends Washington's actions against Iran, describing Iranian leadership as a 'terroristic regime' and asserting US aims to dismantle Iran's ability to carry out strikes across the region

Nearly 500 people killed in Lebanon since the latest escalation between Israel and Hezbollah last week, according to Beirut's government disaster management unit. At least 486 dead and 1,313 injured reported.
events.civilian_impact: Cumulative total: 486 dead, 1,313 injured including dozens of children across southern suburbs of Beirut and southern and eastern regions

events.description: Lebanon's government proposed holding direct negotiations with Israel through Trump administration mediation to end the ongoing war, but responses from US and Israel described as skeptical and unenthusiastic

U.S. embassies and consulates in Pakistan, Jordan, Lebanon, Iraq, Kuwait, and Saudi Arabia temporarily suspend visa services. U.S. Embassy in Islamabad cancels all immigrant and non-immigrant visa appointments from March 9-13.

Iran's Revolutionary Guards successfully control the Strait of Hormuz using maritime insurance as economic warfare. War-risk insurance premiums for Gulf transit have surged and major insurers halt or cut coverage in Strait of Hormuz.
events.civilian_impact: Global energy flows disrupted; approximately 20% of world's petroleum and LNG transits blocked

events.description: 191 people in Israel admitted to hospitals for injuries related to the US-Israeli war on Iran in the last 24 hours, including 1 critical injury, 3 severe, 2 moderate, 172 mild, 10 for anxiety and 3 for medical assessment.
events.civilian_impact: 191 civilians injured

events.description: Iran's foreign minister states Iran will continue missile attacks for 'as long as it takes' in response to US-Israeli operations.
Trump-Putin call signals possible Russian oil sanctions relief amid Iran war, described by Kremlin as 'frank and businesslike' conversation lasting about an hour.

US Tomahawk missile struck an IRGC compound adjacent to a school in Minab in southern Iran, killing an estimated 175 people, mostly children, at a girls school. Verified by The New York Times investigative journalists.
events.civilian_impact: 175 people killed, mostly children at girls school

events.description: Israeli and US wide-scale strikes targeted missile sites, security centres, IRGC and Basij facilities across Iran.
Petrol stations in Tehran began rationing fuel after Israeli strikes on oil depots, with kilometre-long queues forming.
events.civilian_impact: Fuel rationing affecting civilian population

events.description: The Strait of Hormuz effectively shut down by war, chokepoint for a fifth of global oil supply.
events.civilian_impact: Global oil supply disruption

events.description: Iran warned security in the Strait of Hormuz cannot be restored while the war continues, raising concerns over global oil supplies and financial markets.

Israeli airstrikes and ground operations drive hundreds of thousands of civilians from their homes in southern Lebanon and the Bekaa Valley. Rayak Hospital CEO reports shortage of staff and panic, with many injuries and deaths from bombing that began after March 2 evacuation orders.
events.civilian_impact: Hundreds of thousands displaced, many injured and killed, hospital staff shortage

events.description: Death toll from Israeli strikes on Lebanon rises to 486.
events.civilian_impact: 486 deaths cumulative total from Israeli strikes

events.description: Syrian President Ahmad Al-Sharaa said Damascus supports Lebanon's efforts to disarm Hezbollah and backs serious steps taken by Iraq and Lebanon to keep conflict away from their countries, warning current escalation poses existential threat to entire region.

Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei named as Iran's new supreme leader in defiance of Trump threats. He is seen as more hard-line than his father, has close ties to Revolutionary Guard, and will be in charge of Iran's armed forces and decisions on conflict.
White House said Trump was reviewing 'all credible options' on politically sensitive issue of oil prices, which have surged because of the war.
Iranian strike on high-rise residential building in Manama, Bahrain killed a 29-year-old Bahraini woman and injured several others
events.civilian_impact: 1 woman killed, 29 years old; several others injured in residential building strike

events.description: Gaza food prices surge and shortages deepen as crossing closures are exacerbated by the US-Israel war on Iran; families rushing to markets buying whatever food they can afford as supplies become scarce
events.civilian_impact: Food price inflation, market shortages, increased hardship for Gaza population dependent on fragile aid lifelines

events.description: Israeli military carried out several wide-scale waves of strikes across Iran while Mojtaba Khamenei appointed as new Supreme Leader of Iran, succeeding his father Ayatollah Ali Khamenei who was killed in strikes at start of war
events.civilian_impact: None specified in event; political succession during ongoing strikes

events.description: Iran launched new barrages of missile and drone attacks at Gulf Arab states hosting US military bases and Israel
events.civilian_impact: One person killed in Israel (per source); broader regional impact on Gulf Arab states

events.description: Crude oil prices surged to more than $100 per barrel on Monday due to Iran war disrupting global energy markets and trade through critical shipping routes
events.civilian_impact: Global economic impact; energy prices affecting households and economies worldwide

events.description: US stock market indexes dropped and oil prices surged on day 10 of Operation Epic Fury; President Trump made multiple contradictory statements about war timeline claiming it will end 'very soon' but not this week, and that operation is 'pretty much complete' but 'ahead of schedule'
events.civilian_impact: Market volatility affecting retirement accounts and investor confidence; economic uncertainty

events.description: US Congressman Andy Ogles sparked widespread condemnation after declaring on social media that Muslims have no place in American society; sharp increase in anti-Muslim content on social media reported since Israel-US war on Iran began
events.civilian_impact: Islamophobic rhetoric increase; threat to Muslim-American communities; democratic controversy

events.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi stated that negotiating or talking with the US is no longer on the agenda after previous bitter experience with failed nuclear talks, citing US promises not to attack followed by military strikes
events.civilian_impact: Diplomatic breakdown eliminates possibility of negotiated settlement; war escalation more likely

events.description: Oil prices briefly surged near $120 per barrel on Monday and shipping through Strait of Hormuz brought to a standstill; some Gulf countries begun cutting oil production as they run out of storage amid disruptions
events.civilian_impact: Global energy crisis; shipping disruptions; economic impact on oil-dependent economies and consumers

events.description: RAF fighter jets intercepted a drone heading towards Bahrain and took out an uncrewed aerial system to defend Jordan overnight. Fragments of a drone that struck the UK base at Akrotiri, Cyprus, were being examined.
Drone struck the UK military base at Akrotiri, Cyprus. Fragments being examined for foreign military equipment.

Israeli military issued warning of imminent strikes on Hezbollah positions in the coastal cities of Tyre and Sidon in south Lebanon. Residents urged to evacuate at least 300 meters from targeted areas.
events.civilian_impact: Evacuation warning issued to residents

events.description: India activated emergency measures to redirect natural gas supplies to priority sectors after liquefied natural gas shipments through the Strait of Hormuz were disrupted. Gas diverted away from non-essential users due to closure of strategic waterway.
events.civilian_impact: Gas supply disruptions affecting non-essential sectors

events.description: US President Donald Trump reportedly told Wall Street Journal he is open to killing new Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei if he does not cede to US demands. Trump previously called Khamenei a 'lightweight' and 'unacceptable' choice.

US President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin held a phone call to discuss the Iran war and other issues. Kremlin described the conversation as 'frank and businesslike' lasting about one hour.

Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated Iran would keep fighting as long as necessary and ruled out negotiations with Washington, casting doubt on Trump's assertions that the conflict would end 'soon'. Iran launched new wave of attacks on US-allied Gulf nations.

Oil prices jumped significantly and markets slid as Iran named new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei and dug in for continued conflict. Oil prices and market volatility escalated in response to leadership succession and military developments.

UAE's first ambassador to India Hussain Hassan Mirza stated the UAE is not interested in getting involved in the Iran-Israel conflict and will not allow its territory to be used as a launching pad for attacks by either side. UAE announced it will play a neutral role given its geopolitical position as neighbor to Iran and partner to Israel under Abraham Accords.
Cyber warfare tactics increasingly used in Iran conflict, including hacking phone apps, recruiting agents online, and embracing AI as a weapon.
Lebanon expressed readiness to hold talks with Israel to end the fighting as the war in Iran intensifies and regional spillover increases.

Hezbollah launched a volley of missiles and drones towards an Israeli military site in the northern city of Haifa in response to the killing of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on February 28.
Israel carried out a fierce air campaign on Lebanon, killing nearly 500 people including 83 children and forcing half a million from their homes.
events.civilian_impact: Nearly 500 people killed including 83 children, 500,000 displaced from homes

events.description: Iranian forces carried out a drone attack targeting energy infrastructure in the Israeli city of Haifa, striking oil and gas refineries and fuel depots in retaliation for Israeli attacks on Iranian oil storage facilities.
An Israeli man died from wounds sustained during an Iranian missile strike on central Israel earlier in the week.
events.civilian_impact: 1 civilian death

events.description: U.S. forces crippled or destroyed 50+ Iranian ships as naval war escalates in the Persian Gulf region.

Iran's Assembly of Experts appointed Mojtaba Khamenei, son of assassinated Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, as Iran's new supreme leader despite US President Trump's earlier warnings against this selection.

US President Donald Trump told the New York Post that the United States has not made a decision on sending troops into Iran to secure enriched uranium from nuclear sites, stating 'We're nowhere near it.'

Trilateral meeting between French President Macron, Greek Prime Minister Mitsotakis, and Cyprus President Christodoulides in Paphos to discuss Middle East security situation, with Cyprus emphasizing that its security is Europe's security.

Russian President Vladimir Putin and US President Donald Trump held a one-hour phone call described as 'frank and constructive' to discuss the ongoing Iran war and efforts to reach a political settlement in Ukraine.
Toddler's body pulled from rubble after US-Israeli strikes hit Tehran residential area; wave of strikes killed at least 40 civilians in the capital
events.civilian_impact: At least 40 civilians killed including infant girl pulled from residential rubble

events.description: US military reported striking more than 5,000 targets in Iran so far and damaged or destroyed more than 50 Iranian vessels as of March 10
Israeli military conducted precise airstrike targeting key commanders of IRGC's Quds Force Lebanon Corps who operated in Beirut
President Donald Trump had a call with Russian President Vladimir Putin to discuss the Iran war and other issues; Kremlin described conversation as 'frank and businesslike' lasting about an hour
Air strike kills four Iran-linked fighters in Iraq
Iran's Revolutionary Guards target US base in Kurdistan region, Iraq
Israel strikes southern Lebanon; war displaces 100,000 in single day
events.civilian_impact: 100,000 people displaced in single day

events.description: Turkish Foreign Minister warns Iranian counterpart that violating Istanbul's airspace by Iranian ballistic missiles is unacceptable; Iran reports conducting investigation into missile incident

Iran effectively shuts Strait of Hormuz; only two non-Iran/Russia-linked vessels made 'chicken run' through strait since Trump's announcement; Iran attacked at least 10 ships seeking to traverse strait in early days of crisis
events.civilian_impact: Shipping disruption; global oil supply threatened

events.description: Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf says Iran is not seeking ceasefire and must strike the aggressor in response to US-Israeli attacks
Ukraine sending drone team and interceptor drones to help protect US military bases in Jordan at Washington's request, per President Zelenskyy
Trump and Putin discuss Iran war during phone call; Kremlin reports 'frank and businesslike' conversation lasting about an hour
Dozens of civilians, including children, wounded by Iranian drone strike in Bahrain
events.civilian_impact: Dozens of civilians including children wounded

events.description: France deploying warships to secure shipping commerce in Strait of Hormuz
US State Department reports attacks in Saudi Arabia that killed workers from India and Bangladesh; orders nonessential staff to evacuate
events.civilian_impact: Workers from India and Bangladesh killed

events.description: Lebanon reporting half a million people displaced by fighting between Israel and Hezbollah
events.civilian_impact: 500,000 people displaced

events.description: Trump predicts US military operations in Iran would be ending soon; describes war as 'very complete, pretty much' and hints end is in sight
Trump calls the war on Iran a 'short-term excursion', says US is ahead of schedule and already achieved 'tremendous success', but notes operations could continue
Trump threatens to escalate war with Iran if it blocks oil shipments from Middle East
Trump says US has inflicted serious damage on Iran's air force and navy; predicts conflict will end well before initial four-week timeframe
Iranian missile struck central Israel, killing two people and wounding two others.
events.civilian_impact: 2 killed, 2 wounded

events.description: Hezbollah rockets struck Israel hours after Iranian missile barrage, injuring 16 people and damaging a daycare centre.
events.civilian_impact: 16 injured, daycare centre damaged

events.description: Qatari Foreign Ministry spokesman Majid al-Ansari stated that Iranian attacks targeted Qatar and affected Qatari economy and global economy. Qatar was hit along with UAE and Bahrain.
events.civilian_impact: Qatar economy affected

events.description: Iranian attacks on UAE and Bahrain confirmed by Qatari Foreign Ministry spokesman.
Iranian attacks on Bahrain confirmed by Qatari Foreign Ministry spokesman.

US President Donald Trump stated in CBS News interview that the U.S.-Israel military campaign against Iran under Operation Epic Fury is 'very far ahead of schedule' and declared Iran's military infrastructure largely destroyed.
Total death toll in Israel climbed to 12 on the 10th day of conflict. Tehran launched new wave of attacks under leadership of new supreme leader.
events.civilian_impact: Cumulative death toll in Israel 12

events.description: US President Donald Trump told The New York Post that he is not sending ground troops to safeguard nuclear material at Iran's Isfahan nuclear facility and expressed dissatisfaction with Iran naming new supreme leader.

U.S. President Donald Trump held a phone call with Russian President Vladimir Putin to discuss the war in Iran and other issues. The Kremlin described the conversation as 'frank and businesslike' lasting about an hour.

Iranian state TV announced that Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei was named as new supreme leader in defiance of Trump's threats. Khamenei is characterized as more hard-line than his deceased father and will be in charge of Iran's armed forces.
UNICEF reported that 83 children were killed and 254 injured amid escalating hostilities in Lebanon.
events.civilian_impact: 83 children killed, 254 injured

events.description: President Trump declared that the U.S.-Israel war on Iran is 'very far ahead of schedule' and expressed confidence in swift progress of Operation Epic Fury.
Pakistan announced it is sending navy escort vessels for Pakistani ships transiting the Middle East due to the Iran war.
President Trump stated that the U.S. has begun striking Iran's drone facilities and locations where Iranian missiles are made and delivered.
UAE Ministry of Defense reported that its air defense systems are dealing with missile and drone threats coming from Iran.
Qatar's armed forces intercepted Iranian missiles over Doha according to Al Jazeera correspondent report.

Loud explosions were heard in the city centre of Tehran according to AFP. Iranian media reported blasts across several parts of Tehran after Israeli military announced a new wave of strikes on the city.
Israeli military announced a new wave of strikes on Tehran. Iranian media reported that air defences south east of the Iranian capital have been activated.
Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the killed Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, was named Iran's new supreme leader during the ongoing U.S.-Israeli military campaign.
President Trump ordered more than two dozen new B-2 bombers and declared the U.S. military has already struck 5,000 targets in the Iran war, now in its second week.
Iran's Revolutionary Guards declared they would not allow 'one litre of oil' to be shipped from the Middle East if U.S. and Israeli attacks continue.
President Trump threatened to hit Iran much harder if it blocks oil exports from the Middle East.

Egypt faces pressing economic challenges as a result of the Iran war, including record increases in oil and gas prices, decline in Egyptian pound value, disruptions to Suez Canal shipping and trade, and slowdown in tourism.
events.civilian_impact: Economic impact: rising oil/gas prices, currency depreciation, trade disruptions, tourism decline

events.description: President Trump said the war with Iran began because that country was starting work on a new site for developing material for nuclear weapons. Trump stated Iran was 'going to take over the Middle East' if he hadn't acted.

Trump expressed disappointment with the selection of Mojtaba Khamenei to succeed his father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, as Iran's supreme leader. Confirmed that Ali Khamenei was killed in U.S. and Israeli strikes.
events.civilian_impact: Death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran's supreme leader

events.description: Trump says war in Iran will end 'soon' but threatens to escalate, sending mixed messages about whether fighting is nearing an end
Oil markets experiencing steep price rises as Middle East conflict escalates; oil prices could breach $100 per barrel within days amid supply disruption from Iran war
UK HMS Dragon Type 45 destroyer deployment to Cyprus delayed; still at dock after one week with expected additional 5+ days to reach destination to defend British military base from Iranian attacks
CENTCOM reports 5,000 targets struck in Iran during first 10 days of Operation Epic Fury; releases strike video
U.S. military reports damaging or destroying more than 50 Iranian vessels as naval war escalates during 11-day-old conflict
IDF attacks Hezbollah's Al-Quard Al-Hassan Association in Lebanon
Iran rejects Trump's claims of 'short-term' conflict, stating 'It is we who will determine the end of the war'
Trump warns Iran of retaliation 'twenty times harder' over potential Strait of Hormuz blockade
Trump moves to waive certain oil-related sanctions to curb global price surge amid Iran war

Oil prices skyrocketed past $100 per barrel for the first time since Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine, driven by energy uncertainty from the US-Israel war on Iran beginning February 28. About 20 percent of world's oil comes from Gulf region, with over 20 million barrels transiting Strait of Hormuz daily.
events.civilian_impact: Global food prices expected to rise due to soaring oil costs

events.description: US President Donald Trump stated he is 'thinking about taking over' the Strait of Hormuz to ensure it remains open amid Iranian threats to attack ships transiting the waterway in retaliation for US-Israeli campaign.

European Council President Antonio Costa stated Russia is the only country benefitting from the US-Israeli war on Iran, as global energy prices soar and attention is diverted from Moscow's conflict with Ukraine. War has slowed oil and natural gas flows through Strait of Hormuz to near standstill.
Rescue workers search rubble after strikes hit Tehran neighbourhood, indicating active combat operations and civilian impact in residential areas.
events.civilian_impact: Residential building destroyed, rescue operations ongoing

events.description: Rev. Pierre El Raii, a Maronite Catholic priest and pastor of Qlayaa in southern Lebanon, was killed Monday in a bombing while attempting to rescue a wounded parishioner. Vatican spokesman confirmed his death and Pope Leo XIV expressed deep sorrow.
events.civilian_impact: Priest killed while rescuing wounded parishioner; Pope appeals for end to hostilities

events.description: US Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth announced that today (March 10) will be 'the most intense day of strikes' in the war against Iran as US intensifies attacks. Iran has fired its lowest number of weapons in the past 24 hours. General Dan Caine confirmed focus on three main objectives: destroying missiles and drone capability, striking Iranian navy, and preventing regime attacks on US partners 'for years to come.'

Iranian ballistic missile intercepted by NATO air defence systems near southern Turkish city of Hatay, flying nearly 100 km east of Incirlik air base. Analysis suggests missile was fired from east of Tehran and may have been testing NATO air defenses to target crucial NATO radar base in Malatya.

Second Iranian ballistic missile heading toward Turkey on Tuesday intercepted by NATO air defence systems via US destroyers using RIM-161 Standard Missile-3 (SM-3). Missile reportedly determined to be targeting Incirlik air base.

Ali Larijani, head of Iranian National Security Council, issued strong warning to Donald Trump after US president threatened 'death, fire and fury' on Iran. Trump had vowed to strike Iran 'twenty times harder' if it continued blocking Strait of Hormuz. Larijani responded: 'Beware lest you be the ones to vanish.'

Shots fired at US consulate in Toronto in early morning hours of March 10. Police reported gunfire directed at building around 5:30am with evidence of firearm discharge found. Two bullet holes visible in building's glass doors. No injuries reported. Police investigating incident but have no information on suspect or motives.
events.civilian_impact: Building damaged; attack on diplomatic facility; motives unknown but possibly war-related

events.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz voiced concern that the US and Israel appear to have 'no common plan' for ending the war against Iran. Merz stated concerns that with each day of war, more questions arise about how conflict can be brought to swift and convincing end.

Iraqi authorities exploring alternative export routes for national oil after transit through Strait of Hormuz disrupted by war. Oil ministry spokesperson Saheb Bazoun reported that oil production and marketing severely impacted with several oil shipments stuck at sea.
events.civilian_impact: Oil exports disrupted; economic impact on Iraqi economy

events.description: Poll by Israel Democracy Institute found 93 percent of Jewish Israelis back attacks on Iran (second campaign against Iranian regime in less than year), with smaller 26 percent support among Arab citizens of Israel, resulting in overall national public support of 82 percent. Poll surveyed 599 people.
events.civilian_impact: Public opinion strongly favors continuation of war

events.description: Second poll by INSS think tank (surveyed 805 people) showed public support for war at 81 percent in Israel, with 63 percent believing war should continue until Iranian regime is removed from power.
events.civilian_impact: Majority support for extended military campaign

events.description: Heavy US-Israeli strikes reported in Tehran and nearby Karaj overnight, with residents left in darkness as explosions rocked the cities.
events.civilian_impact: Power cuts affecting residents, ongoing strikes over 10 days of conflict

events.description: Israeli strikes on the town of al-Numairiyah in southern Lebanese city of Nabatieh killed eight members of the same family.
events.civilian_impact: Eight family members killed, including three sisters and one child; one victim Muhammad Ahmad Hamdan died in hospital from wounds

events.description: Israeli strikes on al-Qlailah in Tyre, Lebanon killed two people when targeting a car.
events.civilian_impact: Two civilians killed in car strike

events.description: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun called for internationally mediated direct negotiations with Israel and a truce to halt Israeli attacks on Lebanon and resolve the Hezbollah conflict.
events.civilian_impact: Israeli military operation in Lebanon killed at least 486 people and injured over 1,300; displaced 600,000 people

events.description: Qatar's Foreign Ministry seeking to strengthen defense partnership and security deals with the US and Europe in wake of Iranian attacks on the country.

Iranian security chief Ali Larijani warned via X post that the Strait of Hormuz will be either 'a Strait of peace and prosperity for all or a Strait of defeat and suffering for warmongers' amid Tehran's disruption of the vital oil transit route.

US President Donald Trump threatened Iran with 'Death, Fire, and Fury' over Strait of Hormuz blockade and claimed Iran's military had lost its power. Trump posted on Truth Social threatening to hit Iran 'twenty times harder' in response to Iranian denials.

US President Donald Trump said the war against Iran could be short-lived but left open the possibility of escalation if global oil supplies are disrupted. Trump characterized the military operation as a 'little excursion' to 'get rid of some evil'.

Vladimir Putin and Donald Trump spoke by phone to discuss the Iran conflict and Middle East situation. Putin suggested several proposals to end the war. Call initiated by Washington and lasted nearly an hour.
US B-1B Lancers arrived at RAF Fairford as strikes on Iran intensify.
Israeli warplanes launched violent attacks on Beirut's southern suburbs after expulsion orders were issued by the Israeli army.
Double-tap strike targeted the Shajareh Tayyebeh School, a girls' primary school in the city of Minab, killing 165 people, many of them children.
events.civilian_impact: 165 people killed, many children in school massacre

events.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps announced the 34th wave of attacks against US-Israeli targets using pinpoint missiles and warheads weighing more than one tonne.
Russia's Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov held a phone call with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi discussing the deteriorating situation in the Middle East.
Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps warned that Tehran would determine when the war ends and stated that Iran would not allow oil exports from the region if US and Israeli strikes continue.

U.S. President Donald Trump had a call with Russian President Vladimir Putin to discuss the war in Iran and other issues, described by the Kremlin as a frank and businesslike conversation lasting about an hour.
Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the deceased supreme leader, was named as Iran's new supreme leader, taking charge of Iran's armed forces and military decision-making.
Israel targeted a building in Beirut's southern suburbs reported to be a branch of the Hezbollah-affiliated Al-Qard Al-Hassan financial institution.
Israeli Foreign Minister Gideon Saar stated that Israel is not seeking an 'endless war' with Iran and would coordinate with the US on when to end the conflict.

Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth warned that Tuesday would be the 'most intense' day of US strikes yet, and blamed Iran for civilian casualties by claiming its forces were firing missiles from schools and hospitals.

CENTCOM reported that US forces have struck more than 5,000 targets, destroyed over 50 Iranian naval vessels, and hit several drone factories to degrade Iran's autonomous weapons capability in the first ten days of Operation Epic Fury.

France is deploying about a dozen naval vessels, including its aircraft carrier strike group Charles de Gaulle, to the Mediterranean, Red Sea and potentially the Strait of Hormuz as defensive support to allies threatened by the Middle East conflict.
France reported that drones were intercepted heading towards Cyprus last week during the conflict.
US President Donald Trump threatened to escalate the war with Iran if it blocked oil shipments from the Middle East, while predicting a quick end to the conflict.
Iran selected Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei as the new supreme leader, signaling a hardline approach continuing the conflict.
Iran launched fresh strikes on energy installations in the Gulf, causing Brent crude oil prices to jump to a peak of $119.50 before falling to around $82 a barrel.
Bahrain oil facilities were attacked; NATO defenses shot down missiles aimed at Turkey.
Two UAE servicemen were killed in strikes during the conflict.
events.civilian_impact: Two UAE servicemen killed

events.description: A strike hit near an Iraqi military base and Baghdad airport.
Israel launched more attacks on Tehran and Isfahan as Iran pledged bigger warheads in response.
US President Donald Trump said the Iran war was 'very complete' and that Tehran has nothing left militarily, stating the US-Israel were ahead of schedule in terms of the timeline of the war.
Iran effectively shut down the Strait of Hormuz, driving global crude oil prices toward $120.
Protesters blocked a bus carrying Iran's women's football team outside a hotel in Australia. Five players slipped away to seek asylum during the Women's Asian Cup.
events.civilian_impact: Five players defected seeking asylum

events.description: Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth recited a prayer for US troops attacking Iran during a Pentagon briefing, asking for strength and protection.
Iranian Ministry of Health reported that more than 15,000 people have been injured and over 1,200 others killed across Iran since the start of the Israeli-US war, including around 200 children.
events.civilian_impact: 15,000+ wounded, 1,200+ killed including 200 children

events.description: Iran fired drones toward Saudi Arabia and Kuwait as part of continued regional attacks.
Iran launched drone attacks on Gulf Arab countries. Saudi Arabia destroyed two drones over its eastern region. Kuwait's National Guard shot down six drones.
Iranian attack hit a residential building in Manama, Bahrain, killing a 29-year-old woman and wounding eight people.
events.civilian_impact: 1 killed, 8 wounded in residential area

events.description: Iranian drone strike hit the industrial city of Ruwais in the United Arab Emirates, home to petrochemical plants. Firefighters battled a resulting blaze. No injuries reported.
events.civilian_impact: Industrial infrastructure damaged

events.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards stated that Tehran would ultimately decide when the war ends and warned that the country could halt oil exports from the region if attacks by the United States and Israel continue.

US President Donald Trump warned that if Iran interferes with shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, the United States would retaliate far more aggressively, stating Iran would be 'hit twenty times harder' than previously.
Former UAE envoy Hussain Hassan Mirza suggested that Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi could play a significant diplomatic role in easing tensions between Israel and Iran.
Israel expanded Lebanon war front with airstrikes in Beirut.

Iran reportedly issued a possible 'operational trigger' signal meant to activate 'sleeper assets' overseas after the military conflict involving the United States and Israel began, according to an encrypted message intercepted by Washington.
French Navy pledged 10 additional warships to the Middle East to provide escorts for the Strait of Hormuz.
Israeli air strike heavily damaged a building in southern Lebanon's Tyre district. Israeli forces continue to attack across the area, targeting Hezbollah military infrastructure.
events.civilian_impact: Building heavily damaged; residents warned to evacuate south of Litani river

events.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) announced the 34th wave of attacks against US-Israeli targets using pinpoint missiles and warheads weighing more than one tonne.
Iran conducted a drone attack on EU territory in Cyprus, marking the first Iranian drone attack on European Union territory during the war.

Iran's IRGC responded to Trump's 'short-lived conflict' remark, asserting that Iran will determine when the war ends, not the US. IRGC stated the future of the region is guided by Tehran's military strategy.

Iran's Revolutionary Guards threatened to block all oil exports from the Middle East if US-Israeli attacks continue. Trump warned the US will escalate strikes if Iran disrupts global oil supplies through the Strait of Hormuz.

Trump claimed the Iran war could be short-lived but threatened harsher US strikes if global oil supplies are disrupted. Iran appointed Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei as new supreme leader, signaling Iran's commitment to continue the conflict.

Iran's IRGC asserted that it will decide when the war ends, rejecting Trump's claims that the conflict would conclude soon. IRGC stated equations and future status of the region are now in the hands of Iranian armed forces.

Iran's IRGC issued stark warning threatening to block oil exports through critical waterways if US and Israeli military actions continue. IRGC commander Hossein Salami delivered televised address referencing recent airstrikes on Iranian positions in Syria and Iraq.
Nearly 700,000 people displaced by war in Lebanon according to UN assessment
events.civilian_impact: Nearly 700,000 displaced persons

events.description: Heavy bombing reported in Tehran with Iranian civilians expressing concern about destruction
events.civilian_impact: Widespread bombing reported in Tehran

events.description: US Joint Chiefs of Staff Chairman General Dan Caine stated he respects Iran's resistance but does not view Iranian forces as more formidable than anticipated
Israeli military carried out airborne operation that dropped troops overnight in the town of Nabi Chit, Lebanon
Ukrainian President Zelenskyy announced Ukraine will send military experts to Qatar, UAE and Saudi Arabia to consult on downing Iranian drones
Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps announced strikes using Farah, Emad, Khaibar and Qadr missiles targeting Israel and US assets at bases around the Gulf
Pakistan condemned Israeli strikes on Lebanon and warned of deepening regional crisis
Operation Epic Fury: US strikes on Iranian military targets at undisclosed locations
IAEA says no sign Iranian nuclear sites were hit, but Iran disagrees with assessment
Iran's Revolutionary Guards declared they would not allow any oil to be shipped from the Middle East if US and Israeli attacks continue; Trump threatened to hit Iran twenty times harder in response
Assembly of Experts appointed Mojtaba Khamenei (son of Ali Khamenei) as the third Supreme Leader of Iran by decisive vote, signaling hardliners remain firmly in charge

Iran formally shifted to deploying heavy-warhead missiles weighing over one ton; IRGC Aerospace Commander General Mousavi announced new tactics to overwhelm air defenses with saturation attack strategies

Israeli strike on the four-star Ramada Plaza hotel in the Raouche neighbourhood of central Beirut. Israeli military claimed Iranian operatives were meeting inside. Strike occurred without warning early morning, affecting Ramadan crowds in the bustling coastal area.
events.civilian_impact: Civilians and displaced people in area sought cover; strike in central Beirut with civilian presence

events.description: Israeli attack killed four Iranian diplomats in Lebanon on Sunday, according to Iranian state media citing Iran's UN Ambassador Amir Saied Iravani
events.civilian_impact: Four Iranian diplomats killed

events.description: International Energy Agency (IEA) scheduled emergency meeting to assess security of supply and market conditions amid US-Israeli war on Iran. Fatih Birol, IEA executive director, announced representatives of IEA member states would meet to discuss current conditions.

British Airways announced cancellation of all flights to and from Middle East destinations including Amman, Bahrain, Doha, Dubai, and Tel Aviv until later in March, with Abu Dhabi flights cancelled until later in year due to Iran crisis.
events.civilian_impact: Major disruption to civilian air travel

events.description: HMS Dragon, a British Type 45 destroyer, left Portsmouth naval base headed for the eastern Mediterranean to protect Cyprus from further drone or missile attacks.

Russian President Vladimir Putin denied Russia is sharing intelligence with Iran on US military assets in the Middle East during phone call with US President Trump. US special envoy Steve Witkoff made statement on Tuesday. Contradicts Washington Post report from Friday that Russia was providing Iran with targeting information including locations of US warships and aircraft.

French President Emmanuel Macron announced deployment of approximately a dozen naval vessels including aircraft carrier strike group to Mediterranean, Red Sea, and potentially Strait of Hormuz as defensive support to allies threatened by Middle East conflict. Charles de Gaulle aircraft carrier arrived in eastern Mediterranean over weekend.
Drones intercepted heading toward Cyprus according to French President Macron's statement during visit to island.
New footage raises likelihood the US struck Iranian girls' school
events.civilian_impact: Girls' school struck, civilian casualties likely

events.description: Nearly 700,000 displaced by war in Lebanon due to Israeli strikes and bombardment
events.civilian_impact: 700,000 people displaced from homes

events.description: Tehran residents endure heavy Israel-US bombing with warplanes flying at low altitudes overnight into Tuesday, dropping dozens of heavy explosives across multiple neighbourhoods in the capital city of over 10 million people
events.civilian_impact: Dozens of neighbourhoods hit, residents sheltering in homes despite danger

events.description: US spent $5.6 billion on munitions in first two days of Iran war according to Trump administration report to US congressional committees
Trump holds press conference saying the war in Iran is 'very complete' and could end 'very soon', but also claims the US has not 'won enough'
Trump reportedly wants to seize Iran's Kharg Island, an oil export hub, as a way to assert US power in the Gulf and punish the Islamic Republic

Mujahedin-e Khalq (MEK) opposition group announced formation of provisional government tasked with overseeing the fall of the Iranian regime and its replacement with a democratic republic, with 72-year-old leader Maryam Rajavi at the helm
Iran's hardliners staged show of loyalty to new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei (56), a Shia cleric with power base among security forces, in sign that Iran not prepared to back down
Pentagon chief Hegseth says US attacks intensify under Operation Epic Fury while Iranian responses slow

Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) warns it would not allow even one litre of oil to be exported from the region if attacks against Iran continue, threatening to block oil shipments if hostilities persist

Israeli military spokesman Effie Defrin says Israel has killed about 1,900 Iranian soldiers and commanders since start of conflict, also confirms second person killed in Iranian missile attack in Israel on Monday, bringing total death toll from Iranian attacks to 12 civilians plus 2 soldiers
events.civilian_impact: 12 civilians killed, 2,238 people evacuated to hospitals, 91 still hospitalized

events.description: Trump warns that if Iran disrupts global oil supplies, the United States will escalate military action further, stating 'It's going to be ended soon, and if it starts up again they'll be hit even harder'

WHO warns of health risks from 'black rain' caused by Israeli strikes on Iran's oil facilities, posing dangers especially for children. Iranian authorities advise residents to stay indoors as fires and thick smoke worsen air quality.
events.civilian_impact: Health risks to residents including children from toxic air quality

events.description: Israeli strikes pound south of Beirut, displacing more than 100,000 residents in a single day.
events.civilian_impact: Over 100,000 people displaced

events.description: UN Human Rights office raises concerns about health and environmental impacts of Israeli and U.S. strikes on oil depots in Tehran, questioning whether proportionality and precaution obligations under international humanitarian law were met. Sites hit do not appear to be of military exclusive usage.
events.civilian_impact: Toxic pollutants spreading in air from oil depot strikes, 'black rain' and 'acidic rain' posing health dangers

events.description: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright deleted an X post stating the US Navy successfully escorted an oil tanker through the Strait of Hormuz to ensure oil remains flowing to global markets during military operations against Iran. Reasons for deletion unclear.

Tehran experiences worst night of aerial bombardment according to residents. Israeli warplanes conduct wave of strikes including on weapons development facility. Heavy back-to-back explosions reported, fires breaking out, low-flying jets observed above east Tehran.
events.civilian_impact: Buildings shaking, fires spreading, residents sheltering in place

events.description: US spent $5.6 billion on munitions in first two days of Iran war according to Trump administration report to congressional committees. Members of Congress express concern about conflict depleting US military stocks.

Iran launches missiles and drones toward Gulf Arab states. Incoming missile sirens sound early morning in Dubai and Bahrain. Attack hits residential building in Bahrain capital, killing 29-year-old woman and wounding eight others.
events.civilian_impact: 29-year-old woman killed, 8 wounded in residential building hit

events.description: Saudi Arabia destroys two Iranian drones over its oil-rich eastern region during Iranian attack wave on Gulf Arab countries.
Kuwait's National Guard shoots down six Iranian drones during attack wave on Gulf Arab countries.
Missile sirens sound in Jerusalem and explosions heard in Tel Aviv as Israel's defense systems work to intercept incoming Iranian fire during Tuesday attack wave.
Airstrike kills five pro-Iranian militants in northern Iraq during regional escalation. Source unclear but described as occurring during Iranian attack wave on Gulf Arab countries.
Hezbollah missile struck Israeli satellite and radar station near Beit Shemesh, causing extensive damage. Warning sirens reportedly failed to sound in the area.
events.civilian_impact: None reported

events.description: Iran launched drones at Saudi Arabia's oil-rich eastern region. Saudi Arabia said it destroyed two drones over the area.
events.civilian_impact: None reported

events.description: Kuwait's National Guard said it shot down six drones launched by Iran.
events.civilian_impact: None reported

events.description: Incoming missile sirens sounded early morning in Dubai, United Arab Emirates, as Iran launched missiles at the Gulf state.
events.civilian_impact: None reported

events.description: Incoming missile sirens sounded early morning in Bahrain as Iran launched missiles at the Gulf state.
events.civilian_impact: None reported

events.description: UAE detected 1,184 drones from Iran with 1,110 intercepted (93% interception rate). On March 6 specifically, interception rate was 97% for 112 drones detected that day.
events.civilian_impact: None reported

events.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards announced blockade of oil exports from the region to allies of the United States and Israel as long as war continues. Revolutionary Guards spokesman Ali Mohammad Naini said Iranian armed forces will not allow export of oil from region.
events.civilian_impact: None

events.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated Iran would keep fighting as long as necessary and ruled out negotiations with Washington. Comment made as Tehran launched new wave of attacks on US-allied Gulf nations.
events.civilian_impact: None

events.description: Iran's Assembly of Experts appointed Mojtaba Khamenei (age 56) as new Supreme Leader following death of his father Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in US-Israeli strikes. Mojtaba is considered hardliner with close ties to IRGC.
events.civilian_impact: None

events.description: US military drawing up additional options to keep Strait of Hormuz open following presidential directive. White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt stated Pentagon is developing these options and confirmed US Navy has not yet escorted vessels through strait.
events.civilian_impact: None

events.description: Trump administration provided conflicting answers regarding the timeline and duration of the Iran war.
German Chancellor Friedrich Merz expressed concern that the US and Israel have no joint exit plan for the Iran war, warning of dangerous escalation.
Alleged Iranian drone struck the lower floors of a luxury high-rise residential building in Bahrain housing American tourists and US Navy personnel near Manama.
events.civilian_impact: American civilians and US Navy personnel affected, building damage reported

events.description: Seventh US service member died amid escalating conflict with Iran.
Unexploded missile extracted from house in Kuhdasht City, western Iran, following US-Israeli airstrikes
events.civilian_impact: Unexploded ordnance in residential area

events.description: Iranian missile strike on makeshift US operations center in Kuwait killed six US service members
events.civilian_impact: None reported

events.description: Iran's UN ambassador reported US-Israeli airstrikes have destroyed nearly 10,000 civilian sites including 65 schools and nearly 8,000 homes since war began February 28; cumulative civilian death toll stated as over 1,300
events.civilian_impact: Cumulative: ~10,000 civilian sites destroyed, 65 schools hit, ~8,000 homes destroyed, 1,300+ civilians killed

events.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi rejected ceasefire messages from US special envoy Steve Witkoff; Iran signaled willingness to continue conflict and maintain blockade of Strait of Hormuz shipping
events.civilian_impact: None direct

events.description: Iran laying sea mines across Strait of Hormuz shipping lane
events.civilian_impact: Potential threat to international shipping

events.description: US military operations in Strait of Hormuz: Trump announced US destroyed 10 inactive mine-laying vessels; US officials told CBS News Iran may be preparing to deploy naval mines in the strait with a few dozen reportedly laid in recent days
events.civilian_impact: None reported

events.description: Netanyahu addressed Iranian people via X, claiming Ayatollah Khamenei is dead, urging Iranians to remove the regime and stating Israel is creating conditions for regime change and will pass the torch to Iranian people when the time is right
events.civilian_impact: None reported

events.description: Democratic Senator Richard Blumenthal stated after classified Senate Armed Services Committee briefing that Trump administration is on a path toward deploying American troops on the ground in Iran
events.civilian_impact: None reported

events.description: Pentagon reported approximately 140 US troops have been injured in Iran war so far, with 8 severely wounded.

Australian federal government announced it would send specialist surveillance aircraft and stocks in response to US-Israel strikes on Iran, though Labor MPs were privately alarmed about the government's unreserved support for the legally questionable attack.
Reuters reported that as many as 150 US troops have been wounded so far in Iran war according to sources.
War in Iran could lead to food shortages in the region, according to expert analysis cited by New York Times.
events.civilian_impact: Potential food shortages affecting civilian population

events.description: Senate Democrats ramped up pressure campaign for public hearings on war with Iran.
US Tomahawk missile struck next to a bombed Iranian school
events.civilian_impact: School in proximity to strike, civilian casualties unknown

events.description: Iran begins laying mines in Strait of Hormuz according to CNN sources
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announces Tuesday marks the most intense US bombing yet in Iran war, with 11 days of fighting ongoing and Strait of Hormuz effectively closed
Russia reported assisting Iran's military during Operation Epic Fury
South Korean President Lee Jae Myung confirms US is withdrawing air defense systems from South Korea to wage war on Iran despite opposition
Israeli military announced launching massive new wave of strikes on infrastructure in Tehran with explosions heard in Iranian capital
events.civilian_impact: Infrastructure targeted in capital city

events.description: Saudi Arabia ramping up crude flows through East-West pipeline to bypass Strait of Hormuz, expected to reach full capacity of 7 million barrels daily within days
Trump administration reported weighing direct attack on Kharg Island, Iran's key oil hub handling bulk of crude exports
events.civilian_impact: Potential strike could spark broader regional instability and attacks on energy infrastructure

events.description: Israeli Defense Forces launched an additional wave of strikes on infrastructure targets in Tehran. Explosions were heard in Tehran shortly after the announcement.
events.civilian_impact: Unknown civilian casualties

events.description: US military destroyed 16 Iranian mine-laying vessels near the Strait of Hormuz.
Joint Israeli-US strike killed Mohammad Mehdi Farhadi Ramin, a top Iranian cybercriminal on the FBI's most wanted list, in Hamadan.

Democrats emerge from classified Senate Armed Services Committee briefing on Iran war expressing dissatisfaction with White House clarity on war justifications and end goals after 11 days of conflict. Senator Richard Blumenthal states he is 'dissatisfied and angry' with the briefing.
Democrats in US Congress demand that Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Secretary of State Marco Rubio testify regarding Iran war strategy and objectives.
Trump issues warning that any Iranian mines laid in Strait of Hormuz must be removed immediately. President states US has 'no reports' of Iran laying mines despite media reports to contrary.
Iran's Revolutionary Guard announced launch of a new wave of missiles and drones, with reports that Tel Aviv has been hit
US-Israel strike on a Tehran compound killed the elder Ayatollah Ali Khamenei at the start of Operation Epic Fury
US President Donald Trump claims the US has destroyed 10 Iranian mine-laying vessels in the Strait of Hormuz
Iran accuses the US and Israel of targeting civilians, saying more than 1,300 have been killed across the country since the war began
events.civilian_impact: Iran claims 1,300+ civilians killed in airstrikes on civilian sites

events.description: Iran's latest wave of missiles targeted an air base near Tel Aviv; according to Israeli media, a rocket or shrapnel fell nearby a hospital and military base in central Israel
events.civilian_impact: Rocket or shrapnel fell near hospital in central Israel

events.description: Israeli military illegally used white phosphorus munitions over homes in the southern Lebanese town of Yohmor, verified by Human Rights Watch in eight images showing incendiary effects over residential areas and civil defense workers
events.civilian_impact: White phosphorus used over residential area, posed threat to civilians and civil defense workers

events.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards announced they would not allow any oil out of the Middle East until US and Israeli attacks cease; in response, President Trump threatened to hit Iran 'twenty times harder' if it blocked exports
Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps announced it will offer 'free passage' through the Strait of Hormuz to any Arab or European nation that expels its US and Israeli ambassadors
Iran's security chief Ali Larijani dismissed Trump's threats, stating Iran 'is not afraid of your empty threats' and that 'Even those greater than you could not eliminate the Iranian nation'
Lebanon's President Joseph Aoun called for direct negotiations with Israel to end the escalating conflict with Hezbollah, stating Lebanon was ready to negotiate but not while under Israeli fire

Australia's Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke announced that Canberra has granted asylum to a sixth Iranian female soccer player and a member of the team's staff after they faced death threats for refusing to sing national anthem

UN refugee agency reported that more than 667,000 people are now registered as displaced within Lebanon, with an increase of 100,000 in just one day, following Lebanon being dragged into the US-Israeli war on Iran when Hezbollah launched rockets
events.civilian_impact: 667,000+ people displaced, 100,000 new displacements in one day

events.description: Iranian military released footage of missile launches described as a wave of heavy, multi-warhead projectiles targeting Iraq, Bahrain, and Israel. Interceptions were seen in the skies over Israel though reports emerged of a hit on Tel Aviv.
UAE air defence systems attempted to intercept missiles and drones launched from Iran according to UAE Defence Ministry statement on X.

Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) announced the 37th wave of missiles will be the 'most violent' and heaviest since start of war, noting the barrage of attacks will continue for three hours.
Saudi Arabia destroyed two drones over its oil-rich eastern region according to authorities.
Kuwait's National Guard shot down six drones.
Iranian drone strike on Ruwais, industrial city in UAE home to petrochemical plants, caused a fire. Firefighters battled the blaze. No injuries reported.
events.civilian_impact: Fire at petrochemical plants, no injuries reported

events.description: Sirens sounded in Jerusalem and sounds of explosions could be heard in Tel Aviv as Iran launched missile attacks. Israel's defence systems engaged.

Pentagon reported that approximately 140 American service members have been wounded since the start of the Iran war, with eight of them 'severely injured' and receiving medical care. 108 service members have already returned to duty.
events.civilian_impact: 140 US service members wounded, 8 severely injured

events.description: Pentagon reported seven American troops killed so far in the 11-day war with no clear sign of slowing down. US officials indicated the military campaign was likely to intensify.
events.civilian_impact: Cumulative casualty toll: 7 US troops killed

events.description: Iran reportedly laid mines in the Strait of Hormuz, potentially devastating development for the global energy market according to US officials.
events.civilian_impact: Risk to global energy market and shipping

events.description: US strikes Iranian mine-laying vessels as Middle East war intensifies
Hezbollah fired missiles at a gathering of Israeli military forces near the Manara kibbutz in Upper Galilee
Iran vowed to block Gulf oil exports until US-Israel bombing stops, remaining defiant as fresh explosions occurred in Tehran
Iranian television announced launch of a new wave of missiles towards Tel Aviv. IRGC announced the barrage of attacks will continue for three hours.
Iran's police chief issued threat that protesters will be treated as 'enemies'.
events.civilian_impact: Threat against civilian protesters

events.description: Israeli Defense Forces launched fresh 'additional wave' of strikes on targets in Tehran, with multiple explosions reported. Comes after IDF said it had struck key command centres of Iranian armed forces in Tehran and Tabriz.

Hezbollah and Iran launched coordinated strike strategy using cluster munitions in synchronized attacks on Israel. National security experts reported this marked escalation with Hezbollah 'fully joining the war' and well-coordinated with Iran.
events.civilian_impact: Cluster munitions used in coordinated attacks

events.description: Pentagon reported approximately 140 US military personnel wounded in Operation Epic Fury.
IAEA reports damage at Iran's Natanz nuclear facility. Satellite images confirm damage to the nuclear site.
U.S.-Israel air strikes damaged UNESCO sites in Isfahan, including the Chehel Sotoun Palace.
events.civilian_impact: UNESCO heritage sites damaged

events.description: Pentagon Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced Tuesday would be 'the most intense day of strikes inside Iran' with the most fighters, bombers, and refined intelligence. Tehran residents described the war's most intense night of bombardment.
events.civilian_impact: Civilian population experienced most intense bombardment night

events.description: Iran vowed to continue oil blockade of the Gulf until US-Israel attacks end.

Iran vowed that not one litre of oil would be exported from the Gulf while US-Israel bombing continues. United Arab Emirates closed its biggest oil refinery at Ruwais after drone attack as precaution. Drone attack hit the industrial city hosting petrochemical plants.
events.civilian_impact: Major oil refinery closure, industrial disruption

events.description: US President Donald Trump hinted at potential end to Iran war despite Operation Epic Fury not achieving stated aims. Israeli leadership warned abandoned opposition may need to attempt regime change.

Journalists covering Middle East war facing increasing restrictions, censorship, detention, and questioning by governments and armed groups. Iran and Israel imposed tightest restrictions; Gulf monarchies also imposed stricter controls on media coverage.
events.civilian_impact: Press freedom restrictions, journalist detention

events.description: Israel and Iran exchange strikes as missile threats escalate
Israeli airstrikes hit Lebanon as displacement grows and Iran death toll rises
events.civilian_impact: Displacement growing, death toll rising

events.description: Pakistan condemns Israeli military actions in Lebanon, urges international action. Pakistan's Foreign Ministry stated Israel's continued military aggression against Lebanon has resulted in deaths of hundreds of civilians and displacement of nearly half a million people.
events.civilian_impact: Hundreds of civilians killed, nearly 700,000 people displaced

events.description: Iran announces that Arab and European countries expelling US and Israeli envoys will be granted free passage through Strait of Hormuz. IRGC warns Tehran could take broader measures if military strikes continue, threatening to block all oil exports from region if attacks persist.

Iran selects Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei as new Supreme Leader succeeding his late father Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Oil prices surge to highest level since 2022 following announcement, signaling market concern about Iran digging in 10 days into US-Israel war.
US President Donald Trump states the war against Iran could be short-lived but threatens harsher US strikes if global oil supplies are disrupted by Iran, which chose new hard-line Supreme Leader.
Military analysts assess that Israel can manage two-front war effectively

Israel applying 'Gaza logic' to Lebanon with Trump's blessing. Lebanon faces ongoing Israeli attacks with approximately 700,000 people displaced. Israel prepared broader campaign with professed goal of controlling more Lebanese territory and discussions of ground invasion.
events.civilian_impact: Approximately 700,000 people displaced from Lebanon

events.description: Saudi Aramco CEO Amin Nasser warns that further suspension of shipping through Strait of Hormuz will have catastrophic consequences for global economy. Company expanding backup export capacity through Red Sea port of Yanbu and East-West pipeline.

US-Israel war against Iran enters its tenth day with massive bombing of Iranian infrastructure including missile sites and IRGC command centers in Tehran and other regions. Over 5,000 targets hit with total coalition air superiority reported.

Iran's Revolutionary Guards declare they will not let any oil out of Middle East until US and Israeli attacks cease. Trump threatens to hit Iran 'twenty times harder' if it blocks exports. Despite defiant rhetoric, markets show strong bets that Trump will call off war soon.

UN agencies report 84 children killed in Israeli strikes on Lebanon with more than 667,000 people displaced. WHO representative notes high number of child casualties due to attacks on urban centers including Beirut.
events.civilian_impact: 84 children killed, over 667,000 displaced

events.description: US-Israeli strikes hit Tehran near Resalat Square; Iranian Red Crescent recovered bodies and debris from the scene.
events.civilian_impact: 6 people killed, debris scattered at strike site

events.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps claimed it targeted Camp Arifjan US base in Kuwait with two missiles.
Iranian drones attacked UAE's consulate in Erbil, Kurdistan Region of northern Iraq. Part of broader Iranian campaign targeting the UAE.
Iranian drone hit US embassy in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia.
Iranian attack targeted oil processing plant at Ras Tanura, Saudi Arabia.
A projectile fell on a residential area in Al-Kharj city, near a US military airbase in Saudi Arabia, killing two people.
events.civilian_impact: 2 people killed in residential area

events.description: UN agencies report 84 children killed and more than 667,000 people displaced in Lebanon after Israeli strikes, with 486 total deaths and 1,313 injured in Lebanon as of March 10.
events.civilian_impact: 84 children killed, 667,000+ displaced, humanitarian crisis deepened

events.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced that March 10 (Tuesday) would be the most intense day yet of US strikes inside Iran. Joint Chiefs Chairman Gen. Dan Caine stated US military operations had entered their 11th day.

President Donald Trump indicated potential willingness to engage in talks with Iran, provided circumstances and terms are right. Trump expressed strong disapproval of Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei.

President Trump expressed mixed signals about war duration, saying it is 'very complete, pretty much' and could end 'very soon,' contrasting with earlier maximalist rhetoric demanding Iran's 'unconditional surrender.'
Israeli strike on residential building in central Beirut; building caught fire with flames erupting from a hole in its side
events.civilian_impact: Residential building in central Beirut targeted

events.description: US and Israel launched heaviest strikes on Iran described by Pentagon and Iranian sources as most intense airstrikes of the war
Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps launched missiles at Al Udeid US-operated base in Qatar and Al Harir base in Iraq's Kurdistan
Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps launched missiles at Al Harir base in Iraq's Kurdistan region
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced this will be the most intense day of strikes inside Iran; Pentagon providing maximum options to president
Israel's military conducted strikes against the financial infrastructure of the Iran-backed organization Hezbollah in Lebanon
events.civilian_impact: Hezbollah financial infrastructure targeted

events.description: Iran launched drones and rockets across the Gulf region on Tuesday during escalation of Middle East war
US-Israeli war on Iran entered its 11th day as of March 10; countries across Middle East affected with economic losses and casualties; disruptions to fuel supplies causing price increases globally
events.civilian_impact: Economic losses and casualties across Middle East; fuel supply disruptions

events.description: Iran targeted Gulf countries, Turkey, and Azerbaijan with air strikes as part of asymmetric warfare strategy

Hezbollah engaging Israeli troops in south Lebanon using guerrilla warfare tactics; group returned to small unit operations and rationing anti-tank rockets after Hezbollah sparked Israeli offensive last week by opening fire to avenge killing of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
events.civilian_impact: Ongoing engagement in populated areas

events.description: Hezbollah engaged Israeli troops in south Lebanon using guerrilla warfare tactics; group rationing anti-tank rockets and avoiding communication devices; sparked Israeli offensive last week by opening fire to avenge Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's death

Israel stated it aims to eliminate Iran's clerical rule by destroying its nuclear and ballistic missile programs and creating conditions for Iranians to overthrow their rulers; foreign minister said Israel not seeking endless war with Iran
Iran's New Supreme Leader was wounded early in the war, according to Iranian and Israeli officials
Iran's armed forces spokesperson vows response to US-Israeli strikes on residential areas; urges regional countries to indicate US-Zionist positions to maximize precision of Iranian strikes
events.civilian_impact: Strikes on residential areas reported

events.description: US submarine struck the Iranian warship Iris Dena while it was returning from a naval exercise organized by India, killing over 80 people
events.civilian_impact: 84 sailors killed

events.description: Reported airstrike on Iranian girls' school; local officials say 175 pupils and teachers killed; Trump suggests possible Iranian misfire; Sen. Kennedy apologizes on behalf of America
events.civilian_impact: 175 pupils and teachers killed in school strike

events.description: US B-1 supersonic bombers took off from RAF Fairford in Gloucestershire heading to Middle East for bombing missions; at least four aircraft each capable of carrying 24 cruise missiles
British Airways cancels flights to Gulf for weeks due to heaviest US and Israeli attacks on Iran yet
US Energy Secretary Chris Wright deleted social media post claiming navy escorted oil tanker through Strait of Hormuz; Fox News reported well-placed military sources denied any such escort occurred
March 10 marked heaviest day of strikes yet on Iran with heaviest bombardments in the region; combatants renewed attacks on March 11 across Israel, Lebanon and Gulf; war in 12th day
Strait of Hormuz effectively blocked by conflict; vital shipping lanes halted; flow of one-fifth of world's fossil energy supplies from petroleum-rich Gulf disrupted
Lebanon's Hezbollah preparing for potential Israeli invasion by returning to guerrilla tactics
French President Emmanuel Macron to convene G7 leaders to address rising energy prices and Iran crisis

Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei reported safe despite war injuries, according to statement by Yousef Pezeshkian, son of Iran's president and government adviser. State television had called Khamenei a 'wounded veteran of the Ramadan war' but never specified injury details.

Israeli strikes targeted an apartment building in central Beirut, marking the second strike in the city in recent days. Previous strike on Sunday killed five members of Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps.
events.civilian_impact: Apartment building targeted in central Beirut

events.description: Saudi Arabia's Defense Ministry destroyed five drones heading toward the kingdom's Shaybah oil field in the Empty Quarter desert and intercepted and destroyed another two drones in the Eastern Province.
Israel's military identified multiple salvos of missiles from Iran early on Wednesday, bringing the overnight total to four distinct missile waves.
Nine drones struck the United Arab Emirates, resulting in two reported deaths in the country.
events.civilian_impact: Two deaths reported from drone strikes

events.description: Israeli military launched fresh airstrikes on Tehran targeting facilities linked to the Iranian government, including an underground missile-testing complex used by the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) and infrastructure associated with the elite Quds Force.

UN agencies reported that Israeli strikes on Lebanon have killed 84 children and displaced more than 667,000 people. WHO representative stated 'almost 100 children have lost their lives' in the seven-day conflict. A total of 486 people have been killed with 1,313 injured, of which 259 are children.

events.description: Six U.S. soldiers killed as the war with Iran further engulfs the region
Hezbollah fired approximately 200 missiles and drones into Israel overnight and into Thursday in coordinated integrated Hezbollah and Iran joint attack
Israeli Defense Forces conducted fierce retaliatory strikes into Hezbollah strongholds in Beirut suburbs following Hezbollah and Iran missile and drone attack; operation named 'Eaten Straw'
Largest oil disruption caused by Iran war according to International Energy Agency

US strike on a primary school in Iran; Iranian officials claim 168 people killed, including about 110 children; US military investigators believe American forces likely responsible for unintentional strike

events.description: Lebanon: Israeli strikes Beirut in new large-scale attack
