Mojtaba Khamenei, newly appointed Iranian Supreme Leader after death of his father Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, issues first statement in televised address; calls for national unity and pledges Strait of Hormuz will remain closed; threatens attacks on US bases in region

US military confirms use of multiple artificial intelligence (AI) tools in ongoing US-Israel war on Iran; Pentagon collaboration with tech companies including Google, Amazon, Microsoft, Meta and Palantir

Oil prices surge to over $100 per barrel, a third higher than previous levels, driven by airstrikes on shipping and energy infrastructure and effective closure of Strait of Hormuz; Strait of Hormuz normally carries a fifth of global oil supplies; nearly 90% of oil and gas passing through the strait was bound for Asia

Israeli military carried out a wave of air strikes across Lebanon including Beirut after Hezbollah launched about 200 rockets into northern Israel. Strikes hit Beirut's southern suburbs (Hezbollah stronghold), Corniche seafront and other parts of Beirut. At least eight people were killed.
events.civilian_impact: 8 civilians killed in Beirut strikes

events.description: Two fuel tankers were hit by explosions and burst into flames near Iraq's southern port of Basra. Iran stepped up attacks on energy targets in the Gulf.
events.civilian_impact: Fuel tanker attacks, energy infrastructure damaged

events.description: Iranian military declared it has the right to strike financial institutions in the region after an attack on an Iranian bank. Tehran warned that any tanker bound for the US, Israel and its partners was a legitimate target. Iran stated the world should be ready for oil at $200 per barrel.
Israeli military announced it has begun a new 'large-scale wave' of attacks on Iran.

UN Human Rights Council probe warned that Iranian prisoners including detained protesters face expedited death penalty proceedings. Reports indicate prisoners at serious risk of torture, ill-treatment and enforced disappearances.
events.civilian_impact: Prisoners facing expedited executions, torture, ill-treatment

events.description: US and Israeli bombing of a primary school in Minab, southern Iran during military attacks on Saturday. Missiles destroyed a girl's primary school killing around 150 and wounding almost 100. Many students believed to be among the dead. UNESCO called this a grave violation of humanitarian law.
events.civilian_impact: 150 killed, ~100 wounded, mostly schoolgirls and students

events.description: NATO forces reportedly intercepted a missile fired at Turkey by Iran. Iran denied this claim.

Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei in his first statement since taking over from his father stated that the closure of the Strait of Hormuz should continue as a tool to pressure the enemy. He said all US bases in the region should be closed as they would be attacked. He demanded Iran receive reparations for damage or it will destroy enemy assets.
US President Donald Trump threatened to target Iran's electricity production sites if the Islamic republic does not cooperate, warning it would take many years to rebuild such targets.

Iran's Supreme National Security Council Secretary Ali Larijani warned that if Washington targets Iran's electricity network, the 'whole region will go dark' in less than half an hour, and darkness provides opportunity to hunt down US servicemen.

Nepal began rationing cooking gas due to fears of nationwide shortage amid ongoing US-Israeli attacks on Iran. State-run Nepal Oil Corporation announced authorities would refill only half of consumers' empty cylinders to prolong LPG stocks, triggering panic and long queues outside refilling plants.
events.civilian_impact: Gas rationing, consumer panic

events.description: A 60-year-old British tourist from London was arrested in Dubai and charged with two others over videos and online posts related to Iranian missile attacks on Dubai. The man was charged alongside 20 others for allegedly filming missiles hitting the city. He was arrested Monday night and faces up to two years in prison.
events.civilian_impact: 21 people arrested and charged

events.description: Brent Crude Oil price topped $100 per barrel on Thursday, only slightly lower than the $119 peak on Monday. Energy markets in turmoil due to US-Israeli war against Iran in its second week, with focus on Strait of Hormuz as critical energy choke point.
events.civilian_impact: Global energy price volatility, rising household bills

events.description: Iran strikes US-owned oil tanker near Iraq with explosive-laden boat; video released showing the strike. At least one crew member killed.
events.civilian_impact: One crew member killed on oil tanker

events.civilian_impact: 3.2 million people internally displaced

events.description: Multiple explosions reported across Tehran and other Iranian cities on day 13 of US-Israeli attacks. Iran's UN representative reports at least 1,348 civilians killed as of this date.
events.civilian_impact: 17,000+ injured in Iran since war began; over 1,100 children reported injured

events.description: Israeli strike on Beirut's seafront (Ramlet al-Baida area) kills at least 8 people and injures dozens. Area sheltering displaced families in cars and tents.
events.civilian_impact: 8 killed, dozens injured; displaced families sheltering in area

events.description: Three US B-1B Lancer bombers returned to RAF Fairford in UK after first American strike mission against Iran launched from British base. Bunker-busting bombs with JDAM guidance loaded onto aircraft.

German Chancellor Friedrich Merz announces enhanced security measures for Israeli, Jewish and US institutions in response to three incidents since war began, including suspected terrorist bombing at US Embassy in Oslo and blast outside synagogue in Liege, Belgium.
Drone falls near Dubai Creek Harbour building after air defense interception. Minor fire contained with no injuries reported.

German Chancellor Friedrich Merz announces reinforcement of security for Israeli, Jewish and US institutions in Germany due to three incidents in US and Europe; Iran accused of running networks of organized criminals for attacks; three Iraqi brothers arrested in Oslo on terrorism charges

Trump and Putin discuss Iran war during a phone call, according to Kremlin
Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei vows to block Strait of Hormuz in first statement, pledging to avenge blood of Iranians killed in war

Thai bulk carrier Mayuree Naree attacked near Strait of Hormuz with smoke rising from vessel after attack

UAE Minister of State Lana Nusseibeh reports more than 1,800 drones and missiles projected at UAE since war began; Fairmont The Palm hotel and Burj Al Arab hotel damaged; drones landed near Dubai International Airport; thousands of flights cancelled
events.civilian_impact: Hotels damaged, thousands of flights cancelled, civilian infrastructure impacted

Iran laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz according to U.S. officials
Netanyahu claims Israeli attacks killed top Iranian nuclear scientists and inflicted severe damage on IRGC and Basij forces during press conference
Fuel shortages cause long queues and protests around the world as US-Israeli war on Iran disrupts global energy market

events.description: Six French soldiers wounded in drone attack in northern Iraq
events.civilian_impact: Six French soldiers wounded

events.description: Trump states at Mar-a-Lago press conference that Iran war will be over 'very soon' but not this week; claims war is 'very complete, pretty much' but adds it is 'ahead of schedule'

Mojtaba Khamenei, 56, selected as Iran's new supreme leader following assassination of his father Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in first wave of US-Israeli strikes. Large crowds took to streets shouting 'Allahu Akbar'

Iran's UN ambassador Amir Saeid Iravani states Iran will not close Strait of Hormuz but stresses Tehran's right to safeguard the strategic waterway; contradicts earlier remarks by Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei about 'blocking the Strait of Hormuz'

Trump administration under criticism from left and right for flip-flopping on costs of war and Iran's threat; White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt denies Iran poses threat to US 'homeland' when responding to ABC News article citing FBI warning that Iran might strike California coast with drones

U.S. Air Force KC-135 refueler aircraft crashed in Iraq while supporting Operation Epic Fury. One aircraft went down in western Iraq; a second landed safely. Loss was not caused by hostile or friendly fire.
Netanyahu issues veiled threat to new supreme leader of Iran, Mojtaba Khamenei
Air attacks targeted military bases across Iraq belonging to Iran-backed Popular Mobilisation Forces (PMF). PMF members blamed the strikes on the US.

United States Central Command launched rescue operations after losing a military aircraft over Iraq during Operation Epic Fury. One aircraft went down in western Iraq; second landed safely. Loss not caused by hostile or friendly fire.

Trump administration under attack from all sides for flip-flopping on Iran war. White House press secretary denies Iran posed threat to US homeland, contradicting FBI warnings about potential Iranian drone strikes on California coast.
Israeli air strike on the town of Arki in southern Lebanon near Sidon killed at least nine people, including five children, according to Lebanese Ministry of Public Health. Seven others were wounded.

events.description: Sole Muslim woman on Trump's religious freedom committee resigns in protest over firing of anti-Zionist Catholic commissioner Carrie Prejean-Boller. Sameerah Munshi leaves Advisory Board of Lay Leaders on Religious Liberties Commission.

Intelligence arm of Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps urges citizens across Middle East to report locations of US troops via Telegram, characterizing US military personnel as 'American terrorists' and calling reporting an Islamic duty.

Qatar's foreign ministry condemned Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon and called on the international community to intervene and pressure Israel to halt operations in Lebanon.

First day of US-Israel and Iran war: initial report on scope of attacks and their human consequences
events.civilian_impact: Multiple human consequences from opening day of war

events.description: IRGC launched the 44th wave of missile attacks as part of Operation True Promise 4, targeting Israel and US military positions across the region including Kiryat Shmona, Haifa and US Fifth Fleet bases. Barrage included Khorramshahr, Kheibar Shekan, Fatah and Emad missiles.
Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon killed six people: three killed in Ain Ebel during bombardment and three more killed in Barish in Tyre district as Israeli forces intensified strikes.
events.civilian_impact: 6 civilians killed in Ain Ebel and Barish

events.description: US KC-135 refueling aircraft with at least five crew crashed in Iraq while supporting Operation Epic Fury near Iraq-Jordan border. Rescue operations were launched.
events.civilian_impact: At least 5 crew members on aircraft

events.description: Iranian Revolutionary Guards acknowledged that about 40% of its firepower had been directed at Israel, with the majority of more than 2,000 projectiles directed at Gulf Arab states instead.

Twelve independent UN human rights experts condemned US and Israeli military assaults on Iran and Lebanon as flagrant violations of international law, warning the conflict risks expanding into wider regional war.
Thousands rally in Athens protesting US-Israeli attacks on Iran. Protesters marched to the US Embassy to condemn the attacks, joining protests held worldwide over the escalating conflict.
Trump administration removes sanctions on Russia to help oil flow amid Iran conflict.

Iran-backed Islamic Resistance in Iraq (IRI) claimed responsibility for downing a US KC-135 refueling aircraft in western Iraq using air defence systems, causing it to crash. CENTCOM contradicted this claim, stating the incident was not due to hostile fire.

French President Macron announced that a chief warrant officer was killed and several French soldiers were wounded in an attack in Iraq's Erbil region. French troops were stationed in Iraq as part of the fight against ISIS.
events.civilian_impact: 1 French chief warrant officer killed, several French soldiers wounded

events.description: Turkey sends fighter jets to Northern Cyprus amid escalating Middle East tensions.

Turkey Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan stated that Turkey is in talks with both Washington and Tehran to end the Middle East war. He noted this is the 13th day of conflict and described 'the most intense moments of the war in the last few days.' War began February 28 with US-Israeli strikes on Iran.
events.source: Multiple sources (Goldsboro News Argus, Henderson Dispatch, Vincennes Sun-Commercial, Rome Sentinel, The Mercury)

events.description: Iranian missile strikes residential area in northern Israel early Friday morning. Video captured impact in residential area with resulting fire, scattered debris, damaged vehicles and buildings.
events.civilian_impact: Residential area hit, buildings and vehicles damaged

events.description: Attack on building in Galilee region of northern Israel. At least 33 people wounded according to Israel's Channel 12. Emergency services dispatched and rescue teams treated wounded. Authorities reported number of injured rose as more victims found during response operations.
events.civilian_impact: At least 33 people wounded in attack on building

events.description: Iran claims Natanz nuclear site attacked. Tensions escalate over attack on nuclear facility.

Iran's new supreme leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei ordered vital Strait of Hormuz oil shipping lane to remain closed. Message read by newscaster on state television as Khamenei has not appeared publicly since nomination. Statement confirms closure as leverage against US and Israel.

Iran's new supreme leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei (age 56) released first statement since succeeding his late father. Statement vowed Iran would keep up attacks on Gulf Arab neighbors and use effective closure of Strait of Hormuz as leverage against US and Israel. Statement included vow to avenge those killed in war, including in strike on school that killed over 165 people. Khamenei did not appear on camera; statement read by state TV news anchor.
events.civilian_impact: References previous strike on school that killed over 165 people

events.description: Turkey's Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan announced Turkey is in talks with both US and Iran to end the Middle East war as conflict raged for 13th day. Minister stated 'We have been experiencing the most intense moments of the war in the last few days' and called for war to end as soon as possible. Acknowledged ongoing military operations and stated Turkey talking to Iranian side and American side.
Maps and photos show the Iran war has seeped into the wider Middle East, indicating regional spillover beyond Iran and Israel
Trump administration lifts sanctions on Russian oil sales amid soaring energy prices caused by Iran war disruptions

Trump states that Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is still alive in 'some form or another' following reports of injury in earlier airstrike that killed his father Ali Khamenei. Trump claims US military has completely destroyed Iran.
Iran laying mines in Strait of Hormuz surprised Trump administration - reports
US suspends sanctions on Russian oil sales amid Iran war energy disruptions
US KC-135 refueling aircraft crashes over Iraq in non-combat incident during Operation Epic Fury
FBI bulletin warned of possible Iran retaliation using drones in California
Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei calls on Gulf countries to clarify their stance on aggression against Iran and demands closure of foreign military bases
Mojtaba Khamenei vows revenge for crimes against Iran and warns of more attacks on US bases
Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei vows to keep up attacks on Gulf Arab states and use Strait of Hormuz closure as leverage. References strike on school that killed over 165 people.
events.civilian_impact: School strike killed over 165 people (cumulative reference, not single event)

events.description: US and Israeli strikes hit Tehran and nearby Karaj, with explosions occurring every few hours overnight, cutting power to residential areas. Smoke plumes reported near Azadi Tower in western Tehran.
events.civilian_impact: Power cuts reported, residents exhausted from continuous attacks

events.description: Missile strike on a building in the Galilee region of northern Israel injured at least 50 people. Emergency services dispatched and rescue teams treated the wounded.
events.civilian_impact: 50 wounded, building damaged

events.description: US and Israeli strikes hit parts of Tehran on Friday with powerful explosions. Residents across multiple areas reported houses shaking due to blast intensity.
events.civilian_impact: Damage to residential areas, no casualty details provided

events.description: Israeli air strike targeting a residential building in the city of Arak, western Iran killed a woman and a child overnight. City's deputy governor reported 35 other people were wounded in the attack.
events.civilian_impact: 1 woman and 1 child killed, 35 wounded in residential building strike

events.description: Iran steps up attacks on Dubai and shipping as war escalates, with increased military operations against vessels in regional waters.
events.civilian_impact: Shipping disruptions, regional economic impact

events.description: Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei orders the militarization of the Strait of Hormuz in his first public statement. Announcement comes amid claims he is hospitalized in Tehran following airstrikes that killed his father Ali Khamenei on February 28.
events.civilian_impact: Threat to global shipping and oil supplies

events.description: Iran displays its armada of explosive naval drones used to shut the Strait of Hormuz. Regime shows underground tunnels stocked with naval drones, anti-ship missiles, and sea mines. Threatens use of '100 metre per second' underwater missiles.
events.civilian_impact: Disruption to global shipping, 20% of world oil passes through strait

events.description: Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei calls for retaliation for every death in his first public statement. Threatens to keep Strait of Hormuz closed as leverage against US and Israel.
events.civilian_impact: Commitment to continued attacks, regional instability

events.description: India in touch with Iranian authorities to arrange safe passage of almost 30 Indian-flagged merchant vessels through the Strait of Hormuz, effectively closed by Iranian military. Three Indian seafarers killed and another reported missing in attacks on three vessels.
events.civilian_impact: 3 Indian seafarers killed in prior attacks on merchant vessels, 1 reported missing

events.description: United States issued a 30-day waiver allowing countries to purchase sanctioned Russian oil currently stranded at sea to stabilize global energy markets disrupted by Iran war. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent cites supply pressures and rising oil prices following Strait of Hormuz disruptions.
events.civilian_impact: Global energy crisis mitigation, 172 million barrels released from US Strategic Petroleum Reserve

events.description: Iran-linked Iraq group declares French interests in region are targets
Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian lays out terms for ending the war, confirms Iran's commitment to peace after conversations with Russia and Pakistan

Netanyahu says Israel will reach the kingdom and make it to Messiah's return, claims unprecedented gains and reshaping of Middle East; warns Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei and Hezbollah chief Naim Qassem
IRGC claims strike on US aircraft carrier Abraham Lincoln with missiles and drones, claiming significant damage and vessel withdrawing toward United States

Iran's unrelenting attacks on shipping traffic and energy infrastructure in Persian Gulf push oil above $100 per barrel; tanker carrying Iraqi fuel oil damaged and caught fire in Iraq's territorial waters near Basra following unidentified attacks
events.civilian_impact: Tanker damaged, fuel oil spilled

events.description: Iran launches kamikaze drones at coalition base in Erbil, northern Iraq; UK special forces (SAS) shot down two UAVs but others penetrated defenses injuring US soldiers
events.civilian_impact: US soldiers injured

events.description: Somalia warns against Israeli base plans in Somaliland; state minister says Somalia territory cannot be used as launching pad for military operations after reports of Israeli military installation plans near port city of Berbera on Gulf of Aden

Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei warns all US military bases in region must shut down immediately or face attacks; declares Strait of Hormuz closure will continue; vows revenge for those killed and warns of opening additional fronts
Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei calls for continued resistance, vows revenge, insists Strait of Hormuz will remain closed and warns of opening other fronts if necessary
President Trump clarifies administration's primary objectives are permanent dismantling of Iran's nuclear program over economic benefits from higher oil prices
Iran claims strikes on Israeli airbases Palmachim and Ovda and headquarters of Shin Bet using drones in 41st wave of Operation True Promise 4, targeting runways, hangars and surveillance towers
Iran targets world's busiest international airport (Irbil/Erbil airport in Iraq) on Wednesday; major explosion with smoke rising from airport; attack part of campaign to generate global economic pain
events.civilian_impact: Airport attacked

events.description: Iranian attack sparks major fire on Bahrain's Muharraq Island, home to the island kingdom's international airport
events.civilian_impact: Major fire at airport

events.description: UN Security Council demands halt to Iran's strikes on Gulf neighbors that threaten global oil supplies

Israeli airstrike on a residential compound in the western Iranian city of Arak killed 2 and wounded 35 others. Strikes also targeted two production units in the city's industrial zone with no casualties reported there.
events.civilian_impact: 35 wounded in residential compound

events.description: Israeli airstrike on empty warehouses on the outskirts of Hamadan killed 1 person and wounded 5 others.
events.civilian_impact: 5 wounded

events.description: Drone attack on financial centre in Dubai. Debris from successful aerial interception caused minor damage to building facade in downtown Dubai. No injuries reported.
events.civilian_impact: Building damage, no injuries

events.description: Swarm of suicide drones attacked a coalition military base in Erbil, northern Iraq. British special forces (SAS) shot down two UAVs but others penetrated defences, injuring US soldiers. This was reported as the third successful strike against UK forces since the war's start.
events.civilian_impact: US soldiers injured

events.description: Attack in the Erbil region killed one French soldier and wounded several others. Ashab al-Kahf militia later threatened to target all French interests in Iraq and the region following French aircraft carrier Charles de Gaulle deployment to the Gulf.
events.civilian_impact: 1 French soldier killed, others wounded

events.description: Iran's new Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei vowed to keep up attacks on Gulf Arab countries and use the closure of the Strait of Hormuz as leverage against the US and Israel. This was his first public statement since succeeding his father who was killed in an Israeli strike. Khamenei called on people in Gulf countries to shut down US bases.

Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei announced that an Iranian ambassador stated he was injured in the war's opening salvo. Khamenei said the strike killed his wife, one of his sisters, his niece, and his father (previous Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei).

Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian laid out terms for ending the war with the United States and Israel, signaling a possible de-escalation from Tehran. He said he had spoken to counterparts in Russia and Pakistan and confirmed Iran's commitment to peace. This occurred on day 13 of the war.

Israeli strike on a girls school in the city of Minab killed at least 165 people, many of them children, according to Iranian officials. NPR confirmed the U.S. military is investigating how it could have targeted the school.
events.civilian_impact: 165 killed, many children at school

events.description: Iran maintains effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz during the war. Oil prices surge above $100 per barrel (Brent crude up more than 9% on Thursday) amid the biggest disruption to global energy supplies in a generation as traders weigh prospect of weeks or months of turmoil.
events.civilian_impact: Global energy crisis affecting all economies

events.description: US Senator Lindsey Graham stated there is no 'need' for ground troops to be deployed to Iran and that the war will continue for weeks. Graham said the mission is to ensure Iran cannot regenerate and will be beyond capable of building missiles or returning to nuclear development.
Rocket strike damaged approximately 300 homes in the Galilee region of northern Israel, with at least 50 people wounded according to Israeli newspaper Yedioth Ahronoth.
events.civilian_impact: 300 homes damaged, 50+ civilians wounded

events.description: Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei ordered the continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz and demanded the shutdown of all US military bases in the Middle East, describing the strait closure as a tool of pressure against Western powers.
Oil tankers on fire in the Strait of Hormuz, leading to large-scale oil disruptions and spike in Brent Crude oil prices.
Iranian schoolgirls were killed in a botched missile attack according to reports.
events.civilian_impact: Schoolgirls killed in botched missile attack

events.description: Explosion at the US embassy in Oslo, Norway caused minor damage. Three Norwegian brothers of Iraqi origin were arrested on suspicion of a terrorist bombing.
actors: Three Norwegian brothers of Iraqi origin
events.civilian_impact: Minor damage to US embassy

events.description: Two tankers were attacked in Iraqi waters, underscoring risks to shipping beyond the Strait of Hormuz. Oman cleared all vessels from its key export oil terminal as a precautionary measure.
Brent crude oil jumped above $100 per barrel, representing approximately 38 percent increase, amid Iran's continued attacks on shipping routes and energy infrastructure in the Arabian Gulf.

Pentagon officials told senators in a classified briefing that the first six days of Operation Epic Fury cost an estimated $11.3 billion, with the actual total likely significantly higher when additional war-related expenses are included.

White House announced it is considering suspending the Jones Act, a key maritime law requiring American ships for transport between US ports, to address soaring oil prices caused by the Iran conflict.
actors: White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt

events.description: Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei warned that Iran will persist with attacks on Gulf Arab neighbors and may utilize the strategic leverage of closing the Strait of Hormuz.

The conflict involving the US and Israel against Iran has expanded to involve at least 20 countries in some form, including firing missiles, intercepting them, deploying forces, or supplying intelligence and weapons.
Iranian missiles intercepted over Saudi Arabia, Qatar, UAE, and Bahrain. Air defense sirens activated across Gulf states.
Iran threatens to 'set region's oil and gas on fire' if energy infrastructure attacked by US and Israel.
Attacks on commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz intensify. Iran claims it targeted commercial vessels.
Three commercial ships attacked in the Strait of Hormuz amid escalating tensions.

Iran's new Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei issues first public statements vowing to keep fighting, promising more pain for Gulf Arab states, and threatening to open 'other fronts' in the war.
Iran's new Supreme Leader threatens to keep Strait of Hormuz closed and vows to maintain attacks during the war.
Saudi Arabia says a drone was shot down while attempting to approach the Diplomatic Quarter in Riyadh.
Israeli army claims to have hit more than 200 targets in western and central Iran in the past day.
More than 30 people reported injured in an attack in Israel's northern region of Zarzir.
events.civilian_impact: More than 30 people injured

events.description: Drone hits residential apartment in Beirut's Burj Hammoud district.
events.civilian_impact: Residential apartment struck

events.description: Sirens heard at Incirlik NATO base in southern Turkey according to Turkish state news agency.
US carrying out rescue effort after military aircraft crash in Iraq
Israel kills 31 in Beirut strike following Hezbollah rocket attack
events.civilian_impact: 31 killed in strike on Beirut

events.description: Somalia warns against any Israeli base plans on Somaliland
Israeli forces strike Litani River bridge (Zrarieh Bridge) in Lebanon, described as key crossing for Hezbollah movement
Iranian missile strikes impact northern Israel with photographic documentation of aftermath
Iranian attack on US military base in Kuwait injures over 100 US service members; 7 National Guard members wounded and later died
events.civilian_impact: 7 US National Guard members killed; over 100 service members injured including serious injuries in Germany with possible amputations

events.description: Iranian strikes appear to slow down thanks to successful interception by US defense systems; Iran fired 44 projectiles on day 1 and 55 on day 2, but only 5 by March 12
Israel bombs central Beirut; US airstrikes exceed 6,000 total; Iran escalates attacks
Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei says Strait of Hormuz to stay shut; warns of opening other fronts if US-Israel attacks persist
Former Israeli spy warns Iran war means terror attacks in UK are entirely possible; warns of Iranian efforts to use agents and sleepers in Europe

Multiple attacks attributed to Iran strike six ships in Persian Gulf; three ships attacked on March 12 (two in Iraqi port of Basra, one container ship in UAE waters); three cargo ships attacked on March 11
events.civilian_impact: Six ships attacked in Persian Gulf targeting commercial shipping

events.description: Iran reveals underground missile tunnels; new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei pledges to use Strait of Hormuz as tool of pressure against US and Israel
Trump warns Iran not to retaliate after Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is killed
UN nuclear watchdog sees no 'radiological impact' from strikes on Iran
QatarEnergy halts gas liquefaction production following US-Israel war on Iran and disruptions to Strait of Hormuz shipping
events.civilian_impact: Global energy supply disruption

events.description: Kuwait Petroleum Corporation and Bahrain's Bapco Energies declare force majeure and halt gas/oil production following war disruptions
events.civilian_impact: Oil prices soar above $100/barrel; global energy supply disruption

events.description: Israeli airstrikes hit two buildings in the heart of Beirut near Lebanese government headquarters, escalating operations against Hezbollah
events.civilian_impact: Part of campaign that has killed nearly 700 people in Lebanon; over 800,000 displaced

events.description: Vehicle-ramming and active-shooter attack on Temple Israel synagogue in West Bloomfield, Michigan reported
events.civilian_impact: Multiple injuries; active shooter/vehicle ramming at synagogue

events.description: Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei vows devastating revenge for Minab airstrike martyrs, specifically referencing elementary school bombing that killed over 170 people
events.civilian_impact: Reference to prior airstrike killing 170+ schoolchildren

events.description: IRGC warns of harsher response to any renewed protests, threatening stronger crackdown than January incident that killed thousands
events.civilian_impact: Internal security threat; reference to thousands killed in January crackdown

events.description: Millions of Iranians take to streets across country to mark International Quds Day during last Friday of Ramadan, expressing support for Palestinians and condemning Israel
events.civilian_impact: Massive pro-Palestine demonstrations

events.description: Iranian drone strikes building at Dubai International Financial Centre; debris from aerial interception strikes facade of downtown Dubai building
events.civilian_impact: Strike on financial center; debris damage to downtown building

events.description: Somalia warns against Israeli military base plans in breakaway region of Somaliland, stating territory cannot be used as launching pad for military operations
events.civilian_impact: Regional geopolitical warning

events.description: Coalition forces expend over 800 Patriot PAC-3 interceptors during first 72 hours of Iran's aerial attacks across Persian Gulf, costing over $3 million each
events.civilian_impact: Massive military expenditure; defense economic collapse concern

events.description: Israeli Foreign Minister Gideon Sa'ar states Iranian regime is weakening and cracking from internal disagreements since start of US-Israel war
French officer killed in Iraq as pro-Iran group vows to target French interests
events.civilian_impact: French military officer killed

events.description: Nearly 20,000 residential units damaged across Iran; Iran Red Crescent reports 24,531 total civilian units damaged including 4,511 business units, 69 schools damaged with 195 teachers and students injured
events.civilian_impact: 19,775 residential units damaged, 4,511 business units damaged, 69 schools damaged, 195 teachers and students injured, 16 Red Crescent centres damaged, 21 rescue vehicles and 19 ambulances damaged

events.description: China's Red Cross Society provides $200,000 humanitarian assistance to Iran
Israeli strikes across Lebanon targeting Hezbollah result in 687 killed including 98 children between March 2-12 according to Lebanese Health Ministry
events.civilian_impact: 687 people killed including 98 children; pediatric casualties from strikes; wounds described as horrific

events.description: Israel claims strikes have killed top Iranian nuclear scientists; Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei issues first statement calling for closure of Strait of Hormuz and continued attacks on Gulf Arab neighbors
Iranian missile strike on Ali Al-Salem Air Base in Kuwait where Canadian Armed Forces personnel are stationed; Canadian section appeared damaged based on satellite imagery analysis
events.civilian_impact: No Canadian military members injured but airbase damaged

events.description: Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz continues to impact trade through Dubai's Port of Jebel Ali, the Middle East's biggest port. Oil prices rose to $120 per barrel. Port has been in the firing line since Iran responded to US-Israeli strikes by attacking Gulf states.
events.civilian_impact: Trade disruption affecting food imports to the region

events.description: Israeli strikes on the city of Nabatieh in south Lebanon. BBC witnessed the pace and scale of bombings with hundreds killed in Lebanon and hundreds of thousands forced from their homes since the beginning of the month.
events.civilian_impact: Hundreds killed, hundreds of thousands displaced from homes

events.description: Thai cargo ship struck by a projectile 11 nautical miles north of Oman. Ship caught fire after being hit. Part of growing reported attacks on ships in the Gulf region as Iran threatened to open fire on vessels crossing the Strait of Hormuz.
events.civilian_impact: Cargo ship damaged, sailors stranded at sea

events.description: US submarine torpedoed the Iranian frigate IRIS Dena while it was returning from a naval exercise organised by India. 84 Iranian sailors killed, 32 rescued and remaining in Sri Lanka.
events.civilian_impact: 84 Iranian sailors killed in military operation

events.description: Israeli Air Force struck the Taleghan nuclear-weapons development compound at Parchin, southeast of Tehran. Site was previously damaged in October 2024 and was being rebuilt. Facility used for advanced explosives testing tied to Iran's AMAD nuclear-weapons project.
events.civilian_impact: Nuclear weapons development facility targeted

events.description: Israel intensifies campaign against Iran's missile and drone infrastructure. Continuation of operations to degrade Iran's drone network capabilities.
events.civilian_impact: Infrastructure destruction ongoing

events.description: Iran's new Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei issues first public statement since start of conflict. Vows revenge for his father (previous Supreme Leader) and other leaders killed by US and Israel, and for every Iranian who has died in the war. Reaffirms commitment to blocking the Strait of Hormuz.
events.civilian_impact: Leadership succession confirmed; continued commitment to hostilities

events.description: Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei warns in first public remarks that Tehran will continue targeting American military bases across the Middle East. Calls for continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz. States Iran considers US military presence a direct threat to regional sovereignty.
events.civilian_impact: Continued military targeting of US bases throughout region

events.description: Iran vows to keep up chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz. Oil prices soar. International Energy Agency warns the Middle East war could lead to 'the largest supply disruption' in oil industry history. US President Trump states defeating Iran's 'evil empire' is more important than crude prices.
events.civilian_impact: Global energy crisis; potential largest supply disruption in oil history

events.description: Iran's Secretary of the Supreme National Security Council Ali Larijani warns US against escalating war by hitting key energy infrastructure. Threatens that if US hits Iran's electric capacity 'within one hour,' the whole region will go dark in less than half an hour, providing opportunity to 'hunt down US servicemen.' Warns war cannot be won with 'a few tweets.'
events.civilian_impact: Threat of regional blackout; escalating rhetoric about regional conflict

events.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu addresses escalation in Lebanon. States Israel will move to disarm Hezbollah 'on the ground' if Lebanon fails to do so. Warns Lebanese government: 'You are playing with fire if you continue allowing Hezbollah to operate, in violation of your commitment to disarm it.'
events.civilian_impact: Threat of Israeli ground operations in Lebanon

events.description: Crude oil reaches $100 per barrel. Iran vows to keep fighting after 6,000 US airstrikes have been conducted.
events.civilian_impact: Global energy price crisis; cumulative toll of 6,000 airstrikes

events.description: Trump administration, citing Iran, designates Sudanese Muslim Brotherhood (SMB) as 'Designated Global Terrorist' and intends to designate as Foreign Terrorist Organization, effective March 16, 2026. Statement contains warning to Iran regarding meddling.
events.civilian_impact: Political designation; regional policy implications

events.description: Canada pledges $37.7 million in humanitarian aid for civilians in Lebanon caught in crossfire between Israel and Hezbollah. Canadian government calls on all actors to immediately de-escalate and engage in constructive dialogue.
events.civilian_impact: Humanitarian aid for displaced and affected civilians

events.description: Israeli drone attack kills two people on outskirts of Shebaa in southeastern Lebanon, according to National News agency.
events.civilian_impact: 2 killed

events.description: IDF Chief of Staff Lt.-Gen. Eyal Zamir states Lebanon has become co-equal primary front alongside Iran. IDF considering broader ground invasion of Lebanon as fighting with Hezbollah intensifies, though officials signal such move may not be immediate.

Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei issues first statement on war, calling for closure of Strait of Hormuz and continued attacks on Gulf Arab neighbors. Khamenei states he was injured in war's opening salvo which killed his wife, sister, niece and father (Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei).
Crude oil prices soar back above $100 a barrel as American and Israeli strikes continue pounding Iran and Iran attacks Persian Gulf shipping and energy infrastructure with no sign of end to war.
Iran trolls Trump with AI-generated LEGO propaganda video
WFP seeks $200 million to sustain food assistance across Middle East due to war impacts; aid operations strained across roughly 10 countries in region plus Afghanistan
events.civilian_impact: Food security situation worsened; complex economic fragility and supply disruptions affecting availability and affordability of food

events.description: Displacement and civilian casualties mount across region; fighting along Israel-Lebanon border intensifies; millions face growing risks from disrupted health services, overcrowded shelters and strained aid operations
events.civilian_impact: Escalating civilian casualties, mass displacement, disrupted health services, overcrowded shelters, children and vulnerable families bearing the brunt

events.description: Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz threatens to destroy Lebanese national infrastructure used by Hezbollah and warns Lebanese government will pay increasing price with territorial losses until Hezbollah is disarmed
events.civilian_impact: Threat to Lebanese national infrastructure and territorial integrity

events.description: Iran's top national security official Ali Larijani, secretary of Supreme National Security Council, attends Al-Quds Day march in Tehran among large crowds despite ongoing US-Israeli strikes
Al-Quds Day march in Tehran; Iran's security chief Ali Larijani and head of judiciary Gholam-Hossein Mohseni-Ejei attend annual pro-Palestine protest despite ongoing US-Israeli war
Iran closes Strait of Hormuz oil route; potential gas price increases in USA due to disruption
events.civilian_impact: Global energy prices affected; economic disruptions

events.description: US submarine sank Iranian frigate with torpedo attack in international waters off Sri Lanka coast; 84 Iranian sailors killed
events.civilian_impact: 84 sailors killed, 32 rescued

events.description: US-Israeli airstrike near Tehran al-Quds Day march kills woman from shrapnel
events.civilian_impact: 1 woman killed by shrapnel during mass demonstration

events.description: Turkey reports NATO air defences intercepted third ballistic missile fired from Iran towards Turkey
Turkish transport minister confirms Turkish-owned ship Rozana allowed to pass through Strait of Hormuz after receiving Iranian permission; 14 other Turkish vessels still trapped
First French military death reported in Iraq Kurdistan region during the war
events.civilian_impact: French soldier killed in attack

events.description: Hezbollah reported to have launched drone that struck UK RAF airbase in Cyprus
US submarine torpedo strike sinks Iranian warship Iris Dena in the Indian Ocean, killing 84 sailors. 32 sailors rescued by Sri Lankan navy.
events.civilian_impact: 84 Iranian sailors killed

events.description: US KC-135 aerial refuelling aircraft crashes in western Iraq. Four of six crew members confirmed deceased. Crash not caused by hostile or friendly fire.
events.civilian_impact: None reported

events.description: Iranian drone attack on Dubai International Financial Centre. Drone intercepted but debris from interception caused fire and minor damage to building facade. No injuries reported.
events.civilian_impact: Financial center damaged, no injuries reported

events.description: Israeli forces widen bombing campaign in Lebanon to target north and central Beirut areas.
events.civilian_impact: Unknown

events.description: At least 22 people killed in pro-Iran protests in Pakistan and Iraq.
events.civilian_impact: 22 people killed in protests

events.description: Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei makes first public statement vowing to keep up attacks on Gulf Arab countries and maintain closure of Strait of Hormuz. Statement references school strike killing more than 165 people.
events.civilian_impact: 165+ killed in referenced school strike (separate event)

events.description: U.S. Forces attack Iranian mine-laying ships near the Strait of Hormuz
Around 140 US service members wounded in Iran war according to Pentagon
NATO confirms interception of Iranian ballistic missile heading to Turkey; third missile shot down by NATO air defences
Israel launches airstrikes in central Beirut for the first time since latest conflict began, displacing hundreds of thousands of people
events.civilian_impact: Hundreds of thousands displaced

events.description: Unclaimed drone attack kills French Chief Warrant Officer Arnaud Frion and wounds six others in Kurdish autonomous region of Iraq. First death of French armed forces member in expanding Middle East war.
events.civilian_impact: 6 soldiers wounded

events.description: Israeli strike kills eight people in Mieh Mieh, south Lebanese village near Sidon; nine others wounded. Building targeted is part of complex where most residents are Palestinians.
events.civilian_impact: 8 killed, 9 wounded, Palestinians among victims

events.description: US military refueling tanker crashes in Iraq during Iran offensive, killing 4
Iran engaging in cyberterrorism campaign against United States as part of war strategy, described as having 'mental, subjective impact'
Air attack struck near thousands of Iranians marking al-Quds Day in Tehran, reportedly killing at least one person during the rally.
events.civilian_impact: Attack occurred during civilian al-Quds Day rally; at least one person killed

events.description: NATO air defences in the eastern Mediterranean shot down a third ballistic missile fired from Iran towards Turkey, according to Turkish Defence Ministry.
events.civilian_impact: None reported; missile intercepted

events.description: US military refuelling plane (KC-135 Stratotanker) shot down in Iraq; four of six crew members confirmed dead.
events.civilian_impact: Military personnel killed; four US crew members deceased

events.description: Iranian Shahed drone killed French Chief Warrant Officer Arnaud Frion, 42, at a military position in Iraq's autonomous Kurdistan region where French troops were engaged in counter-terrorism training.
events.civilian_impact: Military personnel killed

events.description: Israel issued a new displacement order for southern Lebanon, instructing residents within 25 miles of the Israel-Lebanon border to head north, covering major Lebanese cities and dozens of villages.
events.civilian_impact: Mass displacement order affecting residents of major cities and dozens of villages

events.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu stated during his first press conference since the war began that he wants his country's attacks on Iran to help bring about regime change in Iran.
events.civilian_impact: None

events.description: President Donald Trump stated that the United States would escort vessels through the Strait of Hormuz if needed and planned to strike Iran 'very hard over the next week.'
events.civilian_impact: None
